card.description:
## Context
Unit tests exist but integration tests are limited. Need tests that verify full system behavior.

## Test Scenarios Required

### 1. Full Board Lifecycle
create board -> add columns -> create cards -> move cards ->
archive cards -> export JSON -> delete board -> reimport -> verify equality

### 2. Multi-File Scenarios
- Switch between different kanban files
- Ensure no state leakage between files
- Verify file watcher correctly tracks active file

### 3. External Editor Integration
- Set $EDITOR to test script
- Verify edit flow works
- Test editor crash recovery

### 4. File Watcher Behavior
- Modify file externally during session
- Verify conflict detection triggers
- Test "reload" vs "keep local" paths

### 5. Migration Scenarios
- Create V1 format file
- Load with current version
- Verify auto-migration
- Check backup creation

### 6. Sprint Lifecycle
- Create sprint -> assign cards -> activate -> complete
- Verify card sprint_logs updated
- Test sprint cancellation

## Implementation Plan

### Test Infrastructure
- Use `tempfile` for isolated test directories
- Create `TestHarness` struct for common setup
- Use `assert_cmd` for CLI black-box tests
- Mock external editor with script

### Test Organization
```
tests/
  integration/
    board_lifecycle.rs
    multi_file.rs
    external_editor.rs
    file_watcher.rs
    migration.rs
    sprint_lifecycle.rs
  helpers/
    mod.rs
    harness.rs
    fixtures.rs
```

## SOLID Considerations
- `TestHarness` trait for reusable setup (DIP)
- Fixture builders for test data (Builder pattern)
- Each test file focused on one scenario (SRP)

## Files to Create
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/integration/` - test directory
- `crates/kanban-cli/tests/helpers/harness.rs` - TestHarness
- Individual test files per scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >=1 test per scenario above
- [ ] Tests run in CI
- [ ] Tests isolated (no shared state)
- [ ] Test helpers reusable across scenarios
- [ ] Coverage report includes integration tests
card.title: Add integration test suite

card.description:
## Context
Sprint model exists but is basic. No velocity tracking, burndown data, or retrospective artifacts.

## Current Sprint State
```rust
pub struct Sprint {
    id, board_id, sprint_number, name_index,
    prefix, card_prefix, status,
    start_date, end_date,
    created_at, updated_at
}
```

## Missing Features

### 1. Velocity Tracking
Add fields:
- committed_points: Option<u32> - Points at sprint start
- completed_points: Option<u32> - Points marked done
- velocity_history: Vec<u32> - Rolling average data

### 2. Burndown Data
Add structure:
- BurndownEntry { date, remaining_points, completed_points }
- burndown: Vec<BurndownEntry>

### 3. Sprint Goals
Add fields:
- goal: Option<String> - Sprint objective
- retrospective: Option<String> - Post-sprint notes

## Implementation Approach

### Option A: Full Agile (8 points)
Implement all above + TUI charts for burndown

### Option B: Minimal (3 points)
Just card grouping + basic completion tracking

### Recommended: Option A with phased rollout
1. Add data model fields (this ticket)
2. Capture burndown data on card status changes
3. Add velocity calculation on sprint complete
4. TUI visualization (separate ticket)

## SOLID Considerations
- `SprintMetrics` struct for calculations (SRP)
- `MetricsCalculator` trait for different methodologies
- Keep Sprint struct focused on state, metrics in separate module

## Files to Modify
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/sprint.rs` - add fields
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/sprint_metrics.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/migration/` - V2->V3 migration
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui/sprint_view.rs` - display metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sprint tracks committed vs completed points
- [ ] Burndown data captured daily (or on change)
- [ ] Velocity calculated on sprint completion
- [ ] Migration handles existing sprints gracefully

card.description:
## Problem
100-capacity bounded queue silently drops commands when full. User rapidly executing 101+ commands loses data with no warning.

## Impact
- Silent data loss under normal load
- No user notification
- Only discoverable in logs

## Tasks
1. Remove SAVE_QUEUE_CAPACITY constant
2. Change from bounded to unbounded channel in StateManager
3. Update execute() to use unbounded send
4. Add fatal error handling if channel disconnected
5. Add monitoring: track max_pending_saves
6. Log warning if queue exceeds 1000

## Files to Modify
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs`

## Changes
- Line ~66-68: Remove capacity constant
- Line ~170: Change to `unbounded_channel()`
- Line ~335-346: Update send logic with proper error handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queue is unbounded
- [ ] Never drops commands
- [ ] Fatal error if channel disconnected
- [ ] Monitoring tracks max_pending_saves
- [ ] Integration test: unbounded_save_queue_never_drops passes
card.title: Fix Save Queue Overflow - Use Unbounded Queue

card.description:
## Context
The save queue is implemented in `kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs`:
- `SAVE_QUEUE_CAPACITY = 100` bounded MPSC channel
- Uses `try_send()` which returns `TrySendError::Full` when capacity reached
- Currently logs warning and DROPS the save silently

## Current Problem
When queue is full, saves are dropped with just a warning log. This causes silent data loss.

## Implementation Options
1. **Block writes** - Change to `send().await` (requires async refactor)
2. **Drop oldest** - Replace channel with `VecDeque`, evict oldest on overflow
3. **Expand queue** - Use unbounded channel (memory risk)
4. **Backpressure** - Disable UI input when queue > 80% full

## Recommended Approach
Implement backpressure with clear user feedback:
1. Add `queue_pressure()` method returning 0.0-1.0 ratio
2. Show warning in status bar when > 80%
3. Block new commands when queue full (not silent drop)
4. Document the behavior in README

## SOLID Considerations
- Single Responsibility: Keep save queue logic in dedicated `SaveQueue` struct
- Open/Closed: Make overflow strategy configurable via trait
- Extract `SaveQueueStrategy` trait for different behaviors

## Files to Modify
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs` - StateManager save queue
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui/status_bar.rs` - queue pressure indicator
- `crates/kanban-core/src/traits.rs` - SaveQueueStrategy trait (new)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No silent data loss on queue overflow
- [ ] User-visible feedback when queue is filling
- [ ] Configurable overflow strategy
- [ ] Unit tests for overflow scenarios
card.title: Implement bounded save queue overflow handling

card.description:
## Context
Current tests are example-based only. No property-based testing despite complex domain logic.

## Areas Requiring Property-Based Tests

### 1. Migration Paths (V1→V2)
Location: `kanban-persistence/src/migration/migrator.rs`
- Roundtrip: migrate then demigrate equals original
- Migration preserves all board/card/column counts
- Migration never loses data (bijective mapping)

### 2. Card Ordering Logic
Location: `kanban-domain/src/card.rs`, `column.rs`
- Positions are contiguous after any operation
- No duplicate positions in same column
- Reorder operations maintain invariants

### 3. Sprint Calculations
Location: `kanban-domain/src/sprint.rs`
- activate(sprint, days).end_date == start_date + days
- Sprint status transitions follow state machine
- Sprint numbers are monotonically increasing per board

### 4. Board State Consistency
Location: `kanban-domain/src/board.rs`
- prefix_counters always >= max card_number for that prefix
- Cards always reference valid column_id within same board
- Archived cards maintain referential integrity

## Implementation Plan
1. Add `proptest` to dev-dependencies in relevant crates
2. Create `tests/property_tests.rs` in each crate
3. Use `proptest::arbitrary::Arbitrary` derive for domain types
4. Generate random valid states, apply operations, verify invariants

## SOLID Considerations
- Create `Invariant` trait for testable properties
- Each domain type implements `Arbitrary` for generation
- Reuse arbitraries across test modules

## Files to Create/Modify
- `crates/kanban-domain/Cargo.toml` - add proptest dev-dep
- `crates/kanban-domain/tests/property_tests.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-persistence/tests/migration_properties.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/arbitrary.rs` - Arbitrary impls (new)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] proptest configured in workspace
- [ ] >=10 property tests for migration
- [ ] >=10 property tests for card ordering
- [ ] >=5 property tests for sprint logic
- [ ] >=5 property tests for board consistency
- [ ] CI runs property tests with sufficient iterations

cards.description:
2025-10-20 17:35:49
---

The ci woes continue

The current progress is that of 

---

  New Idempotent Release Pipeline

  Two new minimal scripts:
  - aggregate-changesets.sh - Finds highest priority bump type (major > minor > patch)
  - update-changelog.sh - Merges changesets into CHANGELOG.md with version header

  Updated workflows:

  1. aggregate-changesets.yml (on develop PR close):
    - Aggregates all changesets into CHANGELOG.md
    - Single version bump per release cycle
    - Cleans up changesets
    - Commits to develop
  2. release.yml (on master PR close):
    - Compares Cargo.toml version vs last git tag
    - If version changed → publish & tag
    - If version unchanged → skip (idempotent!)
    - No changeset checking, no pushing back to master

  Benefits:
  - ✅ Idempotent: Version is source of truth, safe to re-run
  - ✅ Maintainable: Simple version comparison logic
  - ✅ Single version bump: One bump per release cycle
  - ✅ Changelog preserved: Full history in CHANGELOG.md
  - ✅ Zero race conditions: No pushing to trigger branch

---

Moving cards to and from columns also seem broken

# Steps to Add Your Project to Nixpkgs

## 1. Add Yourself as a Maintainer

First, you'll need to add yourself to the maintainer list in maintainers/maintainer-list.nix with a separate commit titled "maintainers: add <your-handle>".

## 2. Create the Package Expression

Since your Kanban is a Rust project, you'll use buildRustPackage from rustPlatform nixpkgs/doc/languages-frameworks/rust.section.md at master · NixOS/nixpkgs

[default.nix](./default.nix) 

## 3. Determine Package Location

Decide which pkgs/ subfolder your expression should live in, then add your expression under a directory like pkgs/applications/misc/kanban/default.nix or similar

## 4. Add to Package Index

Add an entry in pkgs/top-level/all-packages.nix so nixpkgs can find your package 

## 5. Fork and Test

Fork the nixpkgs repository, make your changes in a local branch, then build from the root of nixpkgs with nix-build -A kanban Nixpkgs/Contributing - NixOS Wiki

## 6. Handle the Hash Values

For the cargoHash, you can initially use an empty string or fake hash, and Nix will tell you the correct hash when you try to build nixpkgs/doc/languages-frameworks/rust.section.md at master · NixOS/nixpkgs

## 7. Submit a Pull Request

Once your package builds successfully, format your code with a Nix formatter like alejandra, then open a PR on GitHub with the title format "kanban: init at <version>" 

## 8. Wait for Review

Be patient - it's normal for PRs to sit for weeks. If you need help, you can reach out on NixOS Discourse or the Matrix channels

## Important Considerations

Read the commit conventions specific to pkgs and include relevant information in your commit messages so others can understand why changes were made 

The maintainers field is for you as the Nix expression maintainer, not the project maintainer. By submitting this package, you're taking responsibility for ongoing maintenance like version bumps

cards.description:
Be able to group cards by sprints. 
Set sprint duration and automatic starting of new sprints

MVP

Board level settings defines sprint duration.

If duration is set, sprint init is possible.

Add Sprint, set start date, assign cards to it.

When end date is passed. Start up a new sprint and uncompleted cards over.

Sprint will have sprint names the same way tasks do. `prefix-<auto-increment>/sprint-name`

Prefix will be set by sprint settings.

Sprint names are derived from user supplied list or explicitly set per sprint (sprint details)

If a list of sprint names are supplied new sprint will consume each element in order

---

There should be a UI panel for sprints. On selected Board hit 's' to see sprint details

Discussion
---

Looking at this feature comprehensively, here are the potential gaps and clarifications 
  needed:

  🔴 Critical Issues

  1. Keybinding conflict: 's' is already used for board settings. Need a different key for
  sprint panel (maybe 't' for sPrinTs, or nested under settings?)
  2. Sprint rollover trigger: When end date passes, what triggers the new sprint creation?
    - On app startup check?
    - On any board action?
    - Background timer (would need tokio task)?
    - Manual user action only?
  3. Active sprint ambiguity:
    - Can only one sprint be "active" at a time?
    - Can you create future sprints in advance?
    - What's the status model? (Planning, Active, Completed, Cancelled?)

  🟡 Domain Model Gaps

  4. Sprint entity needs:
  struct Sprint {
      id: Uuid,
      board_id: Uuid,
      sprint_number: u32,
      name: String,  // format: prefix-<number>/sprint-name
      start_date: DateTime<Utc>,
      end_date: DateTime<Utc>,
      status: SprintStatus,  // Active, Completed, Planned?
      created_at: DateTime<Utc>,
      updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,
  }
  5. Board additions:
    - sprint_duration_days: Option<u32>
    - sprint_prefix: Option<String>
    - sprint_names: Vec<String>  // consumed in order
    - next_sprint_number: u32
    - current_sprint_id: Option<Uuid>  // which sprint is active?
  6. Card additions:
    - sprint_id: Option<Uuid>  // which sprint is this card in?

  🟡 Unclear Behaviors

  7. Sprint name list:
    - Once consumed, is name removed from list or just marked as used?
    - What happens when list is empty? Fall back to just sprint number?
    - Can user replenish the list?
  8. Card assignment:
    - How do users assign cards to sprints? (UI interaction)
    - Can cards exist without a sprint (backlog)?
    - What defines "uncompleted" for rollover? (status != Done?)
  9. Sprint settings UI:
    - Where do users configure duration, prefix, name list?
    - Part of board settings menu?
    - Separate sprint settings panel?
  10. Sprint completion:
    - Is there a manual "complete sprint early" action?
    - Or only automatic when end date passes?

  🟢 Architecture Questions

  11. UI Navigation:
    - Is sprint panel a new Focus mode (like Boards/Cards)?
    - Or a separate view that replaces card list?
    - How to switch between sprint view and card view?
  12. Sprint panel content:
    - List all sprints for board?
    - Show only current/active sprint?
    - Show sprint stats (cards total, completed, remaining)?
    - Burndown visualization?
  13. Card filtering:
    - Should main card list optionally filter by sprint?
    - Show sprint name/info on cards in list?
    - Visual indication of which cards are in which sprint?
  14. Time zone handling:
    - All dates in UTC internally?
    - Display in local time?
    - Sprint rollover happens at midnight in which timezone?

  📋 Implementation Order Concerns

  15. Data migration:
    - Existing boards need default values for new sprint fields
    - Existing cards get sprint_id: None (backlog)
  16. MVP boundaries:
    - MVP: Basic CRUD for sprints, card assignment, viewing
    - Future: Burndown, velocity, sprint reports, analytics
    - Which features are MVP vs future?

  ❓ Questions for You

  Before I can plan implementation:

  1. Keybinding: What key should open sprint panel? (s is taken)
  2. Rollover: Manual or automatic? If automatic, when checked?
  3. Sprint status: Can multiple sprints exist? What are the states?
  4. Card backlog: Can cards exist without sprint assignment?
  5. MVP scope: Sprint stats/burndown in MVP or future?
  6. Sprint panel: Separate focus mode or replaces card list?

  Should I proceed with assumptions and note them in the plan, or do you want to clarify these
  first?

> 1. We move board settings into the edit project ui. 
> 2. alert the user on app start up with a dialogue that start new sprint (y/n). 
> 3. multiple sprints should be allowed in parallel to allow for focused work on distinct parts of a project. yes we want to be able to plan sprints. picking tasks from the card list. The card list will be filtered to the active sprint. Planning, Active, Completed, Cancelled - yes. 
> 6. I wonder if we would need the bi directional relationship between sprint and cards? 
> 7. marked as used. for now. no list leaves the user to have to manually input a name. until a name is available the sprint name is `prefix+<auto-increment>` when a name is available it is like specified before. Yes the users supplies the list and it can be topped off whenever. 
> 8. When a sprint is initially created it is marked as in planning. then we in the sprint details interface can choose from a list of non completed tasks to include in the sprint. Yes cards can exist without a sprint as the plain list they are currently. uncompleted is only if the user makes the explicit choice not to start a new sprint after the rollover date. in such case manual creation of the subsequent sprint must occur. The user defines uncompleted. 
> 9 having a board selected. we press e to open edit project. in there we will have moved the current s, project settings. in the project settings panel we configure. duration, name list and prefix. We will replace the s with sprint details where the sprint itself can provide per sprint overrides for name list and prefix but not duration. Yes there is manual complete action. in the sprint details UI. 
> 11. The cards for the sprint can be toggled to replace the task list. for now we will use the key `t` to toggle between active sprint and the main card list. 
> 12. Tasks list will be toggled to show sprint tasks or not. Sprint details ui will handle all the rest. show list of sprints, select active sprint. stats and burndown we leave for the next iteration. 
> 12 For now, if sprint tasks are toggled just show sprint name next to the Tasks header, like Tasks - <sprint-name>. in unfiltered task list show the sprint name next to the task name, greyed out. 
> 14. All dates are in UTC internally. Display in local time. rollover in UTC. 
> 15. Yes.
> 16. MVP: Basic CRUD for sprints, card assignment, viewing and all specified. think hard and keep planning with me

---

Excellent clarifications! Let me think through the complete user flows and identify remaining
  questions:

  🤔 Critical Flows to Clarify

  Flow 1: Sprint Lifecycle Timing

  User creates sprint:
  - Status starts as Planning
  - Start date: set at creation OR when activating (Planning → Active)?
  - End date: calculated when? (start_date + duration)

  My assumption: Start date set when activating, not at creation. Makes sense for planning
  sprints in advance.

  Flow 2: Active Sprint Semantics

  You said "multiple sprints allowed in parallel" but also mentioned active_sprint_id
  (singular). I think I understand now:
  - Multiple sprints can have status Active (parallel work streams)
  - But ONE sprint is "selected active" for filtering with t key
  - active_sprint_id = currently selected sprint for filtering
  - Need action to "switch active sprint" in sprint details UI

  Confirm?

  Flow 3: Sprint Rollover Details

  On startup dialog: "Sprint X ended. Start new sprint? (y/n)"
  - If YES: Create new sprint with status Active (not Planning), auto-rollover cards where
  status != Done
  - If NO: Those cards stay in ended sprint, user must manually move later

  But: Multiple ended sprints? Show one dialog per sprint sequentially? Or one dialog listing
  all?

  Flow 4: Sprint Name Consumption

  Sprint name list = ["Cobra Kai", "Miyagi-Do", "Eagle Fang"]
  - Track usage with sprint_name_used_count: usize
  - When creating sprint: consume next name from list

cards.description:
## TUI-Service layer disconnect

The TUI crate builds its own store registry and persistence stack independently from kanban-service. This causes real bugs:

- SQLite feature gap (PR #204): added sqlite as default for kanban-cli and kanban-mcp, but TUI's indirect dependency through kanban-service didn't get it. Switching storage_backend json->sqlite in settings fails because the TUI registry only has the JSON factory.

- Duplicated persistence orchestration: settings handler in kanban-tui manually calls migrate_store, validate_and_load_store, replace_store, resets selection, spawns save workers -- business logic that should live in the service layer.

### Refactor goals

1. Move storage backend switching (migrate/validate/reload) into kanban-service as a single operation
2. TUI calls the service, not orchestrate persistence directly
3. Eliminate the feature-flag disconnect -- the service owns which backends are available

I think it only doesnt happen in kanban view!

kanban/crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs, the handle_move_card_left() and handle_move_card_right()

handlers have a critical bug:

In kanban view (ColumnView), the code only updates column selection but never calls refresh_view():

if self.is_kanban_view() {
    if let Some(current_col_idx) = self.column_selection.get() {
        if current_col_idx > 0 {
            self.column_selection.set(Some(current_col_idx - 1));
        }
    }
} else {
    self.refresh_view();  // <-- This is only in the else branch!
    self.select_card_by_id(card_id);
}

This means:
- The card's column_id and position ARE updated in memory
- But the UI is NOT re-rendered to show the change
- The kanban view doesn't redraw with the card in its new location

In flat/grouped views, it works because refresh_view() IS called.

There may also be a secondary issue: the handlers depend on get_selected_card_in_context() returning a card, which relies on the view
strategy having a selected card. If card selection isn't being maintained properly, the handlers wouldn't execute at all.

Plan to Fix

Here's what needs to be done:

1. Add refresh_view() call to the kanban view branch in handle_move_card_left() (line ~349)
2. Add refresh_view() call to the kanban view branch in handle_move_card_right() (line ~405)
3. Add select_card_by_id() call after refresh in both handlers to maintain card selection
4. Verify that card selection is being properly initialized when switching views
5. Test the fix to ensure cards move and stay selected in all view modes

2025-11-01 22:23:04

I think I made a slight mistake during the design of this addition

these are all for use with branch names to specific cards. that we got right.

Here are the overrides with example prefixes

```sh
default prefix (DEFAULT)

|
v

_application settings prefix (APP)_ [future]

|
v

board settings sprint prefix (BRANCH)

|
v

sprint settings prefix (SPRINT)
```

Creating sprints with just the default prefix yields sprints of

```sh
sprint-1/first 
sprint-2/second 
sprint-3/third 
```

Then adding branch prefix transforms these into 

```sh
BRANCH-1/first 
BRANCH-2/second 
BRANCH-3/third 
```

Then assigning the first sprint a sprint prefix

```sh
SPRINT-1/first 
BRANCH-2/second 
BRANCH-3/third 
```

Then creating four cards and assigning them to each sprint

```sh
task1 (SPRINT-1/first)
task2 (BRANCH-2/second)
task3 (BRANCH-3/third)
task4 (SPRINT-1/first)
```

They get the following branch names

```sh
git checkout -b SPRINT-1/task1
git checkout -b BRANCH-2/task2
git checkout -b BRANCH-3/task3
git checkout -b SPRINT-4/task4
```

The branch naming sequence isnt unique for each prefix!

It is a global sequence where as the sequence for the cards should be unique to each

So. To clarify. There is a couple of sequences going on here.

We have one sequence for each new sprint of the same prefix.

We have a seperate sequence for each new card that also uses the sprint prefix.

There is an unfortunate collision in that the sprint names use the same prefix as the cards.

This was not supposed to be the case. The sprint prefix we had from before this feature was supposed to give us a way to customize the sprint name for each sprint sequence. we moved this into the sprint level definition. This was good for the tasks. but bad for the sprint sequence. I think we have identified all the issues here above. Can we come up with a good plan for resolution?
cards.title: we probably should move sprint prefix into sprint level settings

cards.description:
## Context
The current conflict resolution uses `LastWriteWinsResolver` in `kanban-persistence/src/conflict/resolver.rs`. When external file changes are detected via `FileWatcher` (`watch/file_watcher.rs`), the system compares `saved_at` timestamps from `PersistenceMetadata`.

## Current Behavior
1. `JsonFileStore::save()` checks `last_known_metadata` (Mutex-wrapped) before writing
2. If file changed externally, `KanbanError::ConflictDetected` is raised
3. TUI shows dialog: "Keep Local" or "Reload from Disk"
4. Last-write-wins means the newer `saved_at` timestamp wins

## Documentation Required
Create `docs/CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md` covering:
- How conflict detection works (mtime + size + content hash triple-check)
- Scenarios where data loss occurs:
  - Two instances edit different cards simultaneously → one set of changes lost
  - Rapid saves within same second → timestamp collision possible
  - Network filesystems with clock skew
- Why proper solutions (OT/CRDT) are complex for this use case
- Recommendation: "Single-instance recommended for data integrity"

## Files to Reference
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/conflict/detector.rs` - FileMetadata struct
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/conflict/resolver.rs` - LastWriteWinsResolver
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/watch/file_watcher.rs` - ChangeDetector trait
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs` - conflict dialog handling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New docs/CONFLICT_RESOLUTION.md with clear warnings
- [ ] README.md updated with link and brief warning
- [ ] Code comments in resolver.rs explaining limitations
cards.title: Document conflict resolution limitations

cards.description:
## Context
Current `JsonFileStore` loads entire state into memory and serializes full JSON on every save. This is O(n) for both read and write, blocking scalability past ~1000 cards.

## Architecture: Implement Behind PersistenceStore Trait
The `PersistenceStore` trait in `kanban-persistence/src/traits.rs` is the seam for adding new storage backends.

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Schema Design
Create `schema.sql` with tables for: metadata, boards, columns, cards, sprints, archived_cards. Add indexes for common queries (cards by column, cards by sprint).

### Phase 2: SqliteStore Implementation
New file: `kanban-persistence/src/store/sqlite_store.rs`
- Use `sqlx` with SQLite driver (async-compatible)
- Implement `PersistenceStore` trait
- Transaction wrapping for atomic operations
- Connection pooling for concurrent access

### Phase 3: Incremental Saves (Key Optimization)
Instead of full-state snapshots:
- Track dirty entities in `DirtyTracker` struct
- Only INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE changed rows
- Use `updated_at` timestamps for conflict detection

### Phase 4: Migration from JSON
- Detect `.json` vs `.db` file extension
- Auto-migrate: load JSON -> create SQLite -> write all entities
- Keep JSON as backup until verified

## SOLID Considerations
- **Single Responsibility**: SqliteStore only handles persistence, not domain logic
- **Open/Closed**: Add new store without modifying existing code
- **Liskov**: SqliteStore substitutable for JsonFileStore via trait
- **Dependency Inversion**: TUI/CLI depend on PersistenceStore trait, not concrete impl

## Abstraction for JSON vs SQL
Create shared `StoreOperations` trait for common patterns that both JsonFileStore and SqliteStore implement.

## Files to Create/Modify
- `crates/kanban-persistence/Cargo.toml` - add sqlx, feature flag
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/store/sqlite_store.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/store/mod.rs` - export SqliteStore
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/schema.sql` - DDL
- `crates/kanban-persistence/src/migration/json_to_sqlite.rs` - new
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/main.rs` - store selection logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SqliteStore implements PersistenceStore trait
- [ ] Feature flag: `--features sqlite` to enable
- [ ] JSON->SQLite migration preserves all data
- [ ] Incremental saves (not full snapshots)
- [ ] Concurrent read safety via connection pool
- [ ] Benchmarks showing improvement over JSON

## Context
Error handling uses `KanbanError` enum in `kanban-core/src/error.rs` with `thiserror`. Recovery procedures are undocumented.

## Error Types to Document
- Connection(String) - Future: DB/network errors
- NotFound(String) - Entity lookup failures
- Validation(String) - Invalid input
- Io(std::io::Error) - File operations
- Serialization(String) - JSON parse/write failures
- Internal(String) - Unexpected states
- ConflictDetected { path, source } - External file modification

## Failure Scenarios to Document

### 1. Corrupted JSON File
- Cause: Disk corruption, incomplete write, manual edit error
- Current behavior: Serialization error, app won't start
- Recovery: Check for .v1.backup, use kanban export, manual JSON repair

### 2. Disk Full During Atomic Write
- Cause: Filesystem at capacity during temp file write
- Current behavior: Io error in atomic_writer.rs
- Recovery: Original file preserved, free disk space and retry

### 3. External Editor Crash
- Cause: $EDITOR crashes during card description edit
- Current behavior: Edit cancelled, original preserved
- Recovery: Automatic - no data loss

### 4. File Permission Errors
- Cause: Read-only file, directory permissions
- Current behavior: Io error with OS message
- Recovery: chmod fix, check directory ownership

### 5. File Watcher Failure
- Cause: Too many watches (ulimit), unsupported filesystem
- Current behavior: Warning logged, continues without watching
- Recovery: Increase ulimit, use polling mode

## Documentation to Create
- `docs/ERROR_HANDLING.md` with all scenarios
- Troubleshooting section in README
- Error codes table with recovery steps

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All KanbanError variants documented
- [ ] Recovery procedure for each failure mode
- [ ] Troubleshooting guide with common issues
- [ ] Error messages improved with actionable hints
cards.title: Document error handling behavior

## Context
Current architecture (JSON + last-write-wins) fundamentally supports single-user only.
Roadmap mentions HTTP API, collaboration, sync - these require different foundations.

## Decision Framework

### Option A: Personal Tool (Recommended)
Keep current architecture, optimize for single-user experience:
- SQLite for performance (not collaboration)
- No server component
- File sync via external tools (Dropbox, git)
- Document limitations clearly

Implications: Remove collaboration from roadmap, focus on offline-first, simpler maintenance

### Option B: Team Tool
Rewrite for collaboration:
- PostgreSQL or similar server DB
- HTTP API with authentication
- Real-time sync (WebSockets)
- CRDT or OT for conflict resolution

Implications: 6-12 month rewrite, different deployment model, hosting concerns

### Option C: Hybrid
Personal-first with optional sync:
- SQLite locally
- Optional sync to remote server
- Conflict resolution for sync only

Implications: Complexity of both approaches, edge cases in sync logic

## Decision Process
1. Write ADR (Architecture Decision Record) in `docs/adr/`
2. Update README with chosen scope
3. Update roadmap to match
4. Close/adjust related issues

## Files to Create/Modify
- `docs/adr/001-project-scope.md` - new ADR

- `ROADMAP.md` - align features with scope

- [ ] Roadmap updated to match
- [ ] Collaboration features removed or deferred

## Context
Domain layer uses command pattern (`kanban-domain/src/commands/`), but commands are not persisted. Need to evaluate if this adds value or just complexity.

## Current Implementation Analysis

### What Exists
- `Command` trait with `execute()` and `description()`
- Commands for all CRUD operations
- `CommandContext` with mutable refs to all state
- Command descriptions logged to `VecDeque<String>` (audit only)

### What's Missing for Event Sourcing
- Commands not persisted to storage
- No replay mechanism
- No event versioning
- No projection rebuilding
- No undo/redo

### Questions to Answer
1. Are commands persisted? -> No, only snapshots
2. Is this event-sourcing? -> No, just structural pattern
3. Can we implement undo/redo? -> Not without persisting commands
4. Does the abstraction help? -> Provides uniform API, but adds indirection

## Evaluation Criteria
- Keep if: Command pattern enables future undo/redo, improves testability
- Simplify if: Just adding boilerplate without benefit

## Recommendation
Keep command pattern BUT document its purpose:
- Enables uniform operation interface for TUI/CLI
- Provides audit log via descriptions
- Future-proofs for undo/redo if commands are persisted
- Add `Undoable` trait for commands that support reversal

## Files to Analyze
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/mod.rs`
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/*.rs` - all command impls
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs` - command execution

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document command pattern purpose in code comments
- [ ] Add `Undoable` trait (empty for now, marks intent)
- [ ] Create `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` explaining design decisions
- [ ] Decision: keep, simplify, or extend for undo/redo

## Overview

## Why This Migration?

The TUI crate contained business logic (filtering, sorting, searching, export/import) that should be reusable by:
- Future API server

By moving this logic to domain/core, we follow proper layered architecture and enable code reuse.

**File:** `crates/kanban-domain/src/history.rs`

Unbounded memory growth in long sessions. Each Snapshot clones the entire application state. Every command pushes a full clone onto the undo stack with no limit.

Add a `max_depth` field to `HistoryManager` using `VecDeque`, enforce it in `capture_before_command` by dropping oldest snapshots when limit exceeded. Default to 100. Expose `with_max_depth(n)` constructor.

Tests: verify stack never exceeds max_depth, oldest snapshots are dropped FIFO, redo stack unaffected.

cards.description:
**File:** `crates/kanban-domain/src/query/mod.rs`

30 lines of identical sprint/hide-assigned/search filter logic duplicated across `filter_and_sort_cards` (lines 51-95) and `filter_and_sort_cards_by_column` (lines 100-149). Only difference is the column variant adds `column_filter.matches(c)`.

Option A: Extract a `matches_options()` helper function called by both public functions.
Option B: Use `CompositeFilter` from the filter module to build filter chain dynamically (requires implementing SprintFilter and HideAssignedFilter as CardFilter trait objects).

Two public items named `CardFilter` in the same crate — a trait (predicate interface) and a struct (data bag of filter options for `KanbanOperations::list_cards`). Consumers must disambiguate via path.

Rename the struct in `operations.rs` to `CardFilterCriteria` or `CardListFilter`. The struct is only used in the `KanbanOperations::list_cards` method signature so rename scope should be small.

**File:** `crates/kanban-domain/src/search/mod.rs:61-95`

`BranchNameSearcher::get_branch_name` runs on every `matches()` call, performing sprint lookup, string formatting, title slugification, and branch prefix resolution. During interactive search this is 500 string allocations per keystroke for 500 cards.

Option A: Pre-compute branch names into `HashMap<CardId, String>` at search session start, pass to `CachedBranchNameSearcher`.
Option B: Accept the cost — for typical board sizes (<200 cards) overhead is negligible. Profile before optimizing.

cards.description:
Replace the current HistoryManager (which stores N full-state Snapshot clones) with a command-log replay system that stores 1 initial snapshot + N tiny command structs. Provides audit trail of all commands executed.

## Background: UUID Non-Determinism

**Problem**: Create commands (CreateCard, CreateBoard, CreateColumn, CreateSprint, CreateSubcardCommand) call Uuid::new_v4() inside entity constructors. Replaying these commands generates different UUIDs each time.

**What breaks**: Subsequent commands store entity IDs as fields (e.g., MoveCard { card_id: AAA }). During replay, CreateCard generates BBB instead of AAA, so MoveCard references a non-existent entity.

**Solution**: Pre-generate IDs at command construction time. Add an `id: Uuid` field to each create command. In execute(), override the constructor-generated ID: `entity.id = self.id`. All entity ID fields are pub, so this works without changing constructors.

**Additional fix**: TuiContext::create_sprint() mutates the board (sprint counter increment, name list append) OUTSIDE the command. During replay, these mutations don't happen. Solution: move these side effects INTO CreateSprint::execute().

## Commit Strategy — 4 atomic commits

### Commit 1: Pre-generate IDs in create commands

**Files:**
- crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/board_commands.rs — add id: Uuid to CreateBoard
- crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/card_commands.rs — add id: Uuid to CreateCard
- crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/column_commands.rs — add id: Uuid to CreateColumn
- crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs — add id: Uuid to CreateSprint
- crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/dependency_commands.rs — add id: Uuid to CreateSubcardCommand
- crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs — all create methods pass id: Uuid::new_v4()

### Commit 2: Move sprint side effects into CreateSprint command

- crates/kanban-domain/src/commands/sprint_commands.rs — execute() does counter/name logic internally
- crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs — create_sprint() simplifies to raw inputs

### Commit 3: Rewrite HistoryManager to command-log replay

**File:** crates/kanban-domain/src/history.rs

Replace VecDeque undo/redo stacks with: initial_state (1 clone), command_log (tiny structs), cursor (position).
New API: set_initial_state(), record_batch(), undo(), redo(), can_undo(), can_redo(), command_descriptions()
Removed: suppress/unsuppress, capture_before_command, pop_undo/pop_redo, push_undo/push_redo

### Commit 4: Wire up new history in TuiContext, StateManager, and App

- crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs — execute via &commands then move to record_batch()
- crates/kanban-tui/src/state/mod.rs — remove old history methods, add set_initial_state()
- crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs — simplify undo/redo, remove suppress/unsuppress

## Future: Persist command log to disk

Initial implementation keeps command log in memory only (resets on app restart). Follow-up work to add disk persistence:

- Serialize command log into kanban.json save file
- Replace Box<dyn Command> with CommandKind enum wrapping all ~28 types, derive Serialize/Deserialize
- Add command_log and command_cursor fields to persistence format
- Handle forward compatibility (version tagging for command struct changes)
- On load, deserialize and restore command log + cursor position

## Verification

cargo check --workspace && cargo test --workspace && cargo clippy --all-targets
cards.title: Replace snapshot-based undo/redo with command-replay history system

cards.description:
When creating a board, columns are created correctly. However, undoing the board creation and then redoing it causes the columns to disappear.

## Root Cause

Handlers that execute multiple commands for a single user action create multiple undo entries. `execute_command()` and `execute_commands_batch()` each capture a before-snapshot and push to the undo stack. Calling them multiple times = multiple undo entries for one logical operation.

Board creation fires two separate command batches — `execute_command(CreateBoard)` then `execute_commands_batch([CreateColumn×3])` — resulting in two undo stack entries for what the user perceives as one action. On redo, the first entry restores the board without columns.

## Affected Handlers (4 total)

### Needs command group API (intermediate ID dependency)

1. **`board_handlers.rs:create_board()`** (lines 77, 103)
   - `execute_command(CreateBoard)` then `execute_commands_batch([CreateColumn x3])`
   - Needs `board_id` from CreateBoard to build CreateColumn commands

2. **`card_handlers.rs:create_card()`** (lines 349, 371)
   - `execute_command(CreateCard)` then `execute_command(UpdateCard)` when auto-complete
   - Needs `card_id` from CreateCard for UpdateCard

### Simple batch merge (all IDs known upfront)

3. **`column_handlers.rs:delete_column()`** (lines 358, 368)
   - `execute_commands_batch(move_commands)` then `execute_command(DeleteColumn)`
   - Fix: append DeleteColumn to move_commands vec, single batch call

4. **`detail_view_handlers.rs` MoveColumn action** (lines 794, 808)
   - `execute_command(MoveCard)` then `execute_command(UpdateCard)`
   - Fix: build vec with both commands, single `execute_commands_batch` call

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add command group API to TuiContext

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs`

Add `pending_group_snapshot: Option<Snapshot>` field to `TuiContext`.

Add four methods:
- `begin_command_group()` — clone current state into `pending_group_snapshot`
- `execute_grouped_command()` — call `state_manager.execute_with_context()` directly (no snapshot capture)
- `commit_command_group()` — pass stored before-snapshot to `state_manager.capture_before_command()`, then `queue_snapshot()` with current state
- `rollback_command_group()` — restore fields from stored snapshot, clear `pending_group_snapshot`

### Step 2: Add App-level delegation

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs`

Add matching methods on `App` that delegate to `self.ctx`.

### Step 3: Fix create_board handler

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/board_handlers.rs`

Replace the two separate calls with command group pattern:
```
begin_command_group()
execute_grouped_command(CreateBoard)
// read board_id from self.ctx.boards.last()
execute_grouped_command(CreateColumn) x3
commit_command_group()
```

### Step 4: Fix create_card handler (auto-complete path)

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs`

When `mark_as_complete` is true, wrap in command group. When not auto-completing, keep single `execute_command()`.

### Step 5: Fix delete_column handler (simple merge)

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/column_handlers.rs`

Append `DeleteColumn` to the `move_commands` vec. Single `execute_commands_batch()` call.

### Step 6: Fix detail_view MoveColumn handler (simple merge)

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs`

Build a commands vec with MoveCard and conditionally UpdateCard. Single `execute_commands_batch()` call.

### Step 7: Tests

- Test that command group creates exactly 1 undo entry for multiple commands
- Test that undo after command group restores complete pre-group state
- Test that redo after undo restores complete post-group state
- Test rollback restores pre-group state and creates no undo entry

## Files Modified

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs` | Add `pending_group_snapshot` field + 4 methods |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs` | Add 4 delegation methods |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/board_handlers.rs` | Rewrite `create_board()` with command group |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/card_handlers.rs` | Wrap auto-complete path in command group |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/column_handlers.rs` | Merge delete_column commands into single batch |
| `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs` | Merge MoveColumn commands into single batch |

## Architecture Notes

- **No domain layer changes** — HistoryManager already has correct primitives
- **No core crate changes** — no new traits needed
- Command group API lives in TUI layer (TuiContext) where orchestration belongs
- `execute_commands_batch` remains unchanged — command group is an alternative for when intermediate state is needed between commands
cards.title: Board creation undo/redo loses columns due to split undo entries

cards.description:
## Problem

The three interfaces (TUI, CLI, MCP) are not at feature parity. `KanbanOperations` trait in `kanban-domain` enforces compile-time parity between TUI (`TuiContext`) and CLI (`CliContext`) — 36 methods. But the MCP server has a separate `McpTools` trait with only 14 methods, delegates to CLI subprocesses, and has no compile-time enforcement.

## Current State

- `KanbanOperations` (36 methods) in `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs:19-84`
- `CliContext` implements it at `crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs:172`
- `TuiContext` implements it at `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs:92`
- MCP has separate `McpTools` trait (14 methods) at `crates/kanban-mcp/src/tools_trait.rs`
- MCP spawns CLI subprocesses via `CliExecutor` at `crates/kanban-mcp/src/executor.rs`
- MCP crate has **zero dependency** on `kanban-domain`, `kanban-core`, or `kanban-persistence`

## Desired End State

- MCP implements all 36 `KanbanOperations` methods as MCP tools
- MCP depends on `kanban-domain`, `kanban-core`, `kanban-persistence` directly
- `McpTools` trait and `CliExecutor` are deleted
- `McpContext` struct implements `KanbanOperations` (same pattern as `CliContext`)
- Adding a method to `KanbanOperations` causes a compile error in MCP until wired up
- No subprocess spawning, no CLI arg building, no retry logic

## Missing from MCP (22 operations)

**Board**: update_board
**Column**: get_column, update_column, reorder_column
**Card**: restore_card, list_archived_cards, assign_card_to_sprint, unassign_card_from_sprint, get_card_branch_name, get_card_git_checkout, bulk_archive_cards, bulk_move_cards, bulk_assign_sprint
**Sprint** (all 8): create, list, get, update, activate, complete, cancel, delete
**Import/Export**: export_board, import_board

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Add Dependencies and Create McpContext

1. **Update `crates/kanban-mcp/Cargo.toml`** — add `kanban-core`, `kanban-domain`, `kanban-persistence`, `uuid` as dependencies

2. **Create `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs`** — `McpContext` struct following `CliContext` pattern:
   - Same fields: `boards`, `columns`, `cards`, `sprints`, `archived_cards`, `graph`, `store`
   - Same `load()` async constructor via `JsonFileStore`
   - Same `execute()` method using `CommandContext`
   - Same `save()` async method
   - `impl KanbanOperations for McpContext` (copy from `CliContext`)
   - Local `DataSnapshot` struct (same as CLI's — simple 6-field serialize/deserialize)

**Key design**: `KanbanOperations` uses `&mut self`, MCP tools receive `&self`. Solution: `Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<McpContext>>`

**Verify**: `cargo build --package kanban-mcp`

### Phase 2: Replace Subprocess Delegation

1. **Update `KanbanMcpServer`** — replace `executor: Arc<CliExecutor>` with `ctx: Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<McpContext>>`

2. **Update `main.rs`** — `KanbanMcpServer::new()` becomes async (loads data at startup)

3. **Add helpers**:
   - `parse_uuid(s: &str) -> Result<Uuid, McpError>` for String→Uuid conversion
   - `to_mcp_error(e: impl Display) -> McpError` to reduce boilerplate
   - `list_result<T: Serialize>(items: Vec<T>) -> CallToolResult` for consistent list responses

4. **Rewrite existing 14 tool implementations** — each tool: lock mutex → call trait method → save on writes → serialize result

   Read pattern:
   ```rust
   async fn tool_list_boards(&self) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> {
       let ctx = self.ctx.lock().await;
       let boards = ctx.list_boards().map_err(to_mcp_error)?;
       Ok(list_result(boards))
   }
   ```
   Write pattern:
   ```rust
   async fn tool_create_board(&self, Parameters(req): Parameters<CreateBoardRequest>) -> Result<CallToolResult, McpError> {
       let mut ctx = self.ctx.lock().await;
       let board = ctx.create_board(req.name, req.card_prefix).map_err(to_mcp_error)?;
       ctx.save().await.map_err(to_mcp_error)?;
       Ok(json_result(serde_json::to_value(&board).unwrap()))
   }
   ```

5. **Delete dead code**:
   - Delete `crates/kanban-mcp/src/executor.rs`
   - Delete `crates/kanban-mcp/src/tools_trait.rs`
   - Remove `mod executor`, `mod tools_trait`, all `use` of deleted types from `lib.rs`

**Verify**: `cargo build --package kanban-mcp`, existing 14 tools still work

### Phase 3: Add Missing 22 MCP Tools

Add request types + `#[tool]` wrappers for all missing operations:

**Board** (1 tool):
- `tool_update_board` — "Update a board's properties (name, description, prefixes)"

**Column** (3 tools):
- `tool_get_column` — "Get a specific column by ID"
- `tool_update_column` — "Update a column's properties (name, position, WIP limit)"
- `tool_reorder_column` — "Move a column to a new position"

**Card** (10 tools):
- `tool_restore_card` — "Restore an archived card"
- `tool_list_archived_cards` — "List all archived cards"
- `tool_assign_card_to_sprint` — "Assign a card to a sprint"
- `tool_unassign_card_from_sprint` — "Remove a card's sprint assignment"
- `tool_get_card_branch_name` — "Get git branch name for a card"
- `tool_get_card_git_checkout` — "Get git checkout command for a card"
- `tool_bulk_archive_cards` — "Archive multiple cards"
- `tool_bulk_move_cards` — "Move multiple cards to a column"
- `tool_bulk_assign_sprint` — "Assign multiple cards to a sprint"

**Sprint** (8 tools):
- `tool_create_sprint` — "Create a new sprint"
- `tool_list_sprints` — "List all sprints for a board"
- `tool_get_sprint` — "Get a specific sprint by ID"
- `tool_update_sprint` — "Update sprint properties"
- `tool_activate_sprint` — "Activate a sprint"
- `tool_complete_sprint` — "Complete an active sprint"
- `tool_cancel_sprint` — "Cancel an active sprint"
- `tool_delete_sprint` — "Delete a sprint"

**Import/Export** (2 tools):
- `tool_export_board` — "Export board data as JSON"
- `tool_import_board` — "Import board data from JSON"

Also update `ListCardsRequest` to add `status` filter parameter.

**Verify**: `cargo build`, `cargo clippy`, all 36 tools listed when connecting

### Phase 4: Verification

- `cargo build` (full workspace)
- `cargo clippy --package kanban-mcp`
- `cargo test`
- Connect via `.mcp.json`, verify all 36 tools appear
- Test sprint CRUD, card archive/restore, bulk ops, export/import through MCP

## What We're NOT Doing

- NOT extracting shared base context crate (MCP context will duplicate CliContext pattern — acceptable)
- NOT changing `KanbanOperations` trait itself
- NOT adding undo/redo to MCP (TUI-specific)
- NOT changing `.mcp.json` config or CLI binary interface
- NOT adding new operations beyond existing 36

## References

- `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs:19-84` — KanbanOperations trait
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs:39-683` — CliContext (model for McpContext)
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs:17-569` — TuiContext (reference)
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` — current MCP server
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/executor.rs` — CliExecutor (to delete)
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/tools_trait.rs` — McpTools (to delete)
cards.title: Bring MCP to full feature parity with CLI/TUI via KanbanOperations trait

cards.description:
## Bug

When assigning a card to a sprint via the TUI ('a' key), the wrong sprint gets assigned. Cards end up on old Completed sprints instead of the intended one.

## Root Cause

Index mismatch between the sprint selection dialog and the selection handler:

**Dialog rendering** (`selection_dialog.rs:194-202`) filters OUT Completed/Cancelled sprints:
```rust
.filter(|s| s.status != SprintStatus::Completed && s.status != SprintStatus::Cancelled)
```
Shows: `[(None), Sprint #11 "a-new-year" (Planning)]`

**Selection handler** (`popup_handlers.rs:209-215`) uses UNFILTERED list:
```rust
let board_sprints: Vec<_> = self.ctx.sprints.iter()
    .filter(|s| s.board_id == board_id)
    .collect();
if let Some(sprint) = board_sprints.get(selection_idx - 1) { ... }
```
Unfiltered: `[Sprint #1 (Completed), Sprint #6 (Completed), ..., Sprint #11 (Planning)]`

User selects index 1 (Sprint #11 in filtered list) -> handler does `get(0)` on unfiltered list -> gets Sprint #1 "an-eventful-october".

## Affected Code

- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/popup_handlers.rs:209-215` -- single card assignment
- `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/popup_handlers.rs:337-343` -- multi-card assignment (same bug)

## Fix

Apply the same Completed/Cancelled filter in the handler that the dialog uses:
```rust
let board_sprints: Vec<_> = self.ctx.sprints.iter()
    .filter(|s| s.board_id == board_id)
    .filter(|s| s.status != SprintStatus::Completed && s.status != SprintStatus::Cancelled)
    .collect();
```

Both single-card and multi-card paths need the fix.

## Notes

- CLI is not affected (uses UUID directly, no index-based selection)
- MCP is not affected (no sprint assignment tool yet)
cards.title: Fix TUI sprint assignment selecting wrong sprint (index mismatch)

cards.description:
## Context

During the Sprint::assignable refactor (KAN-194 follow-up), we identified a design gap: domain business rules like "only Planning/Active sprints are assignable" are encoded as functions in kanban-domain (e.g. Sprint::assignable), but nothing prevents consumers (TUI, CLI, MCP, future API) from bypassing them by accessing raw data directly.

This is exactly how the original sprint index mismatch bug happened — the UI filtered sprints for display but handlers accessed the unfiltered list.

## Current State

- KanbanOperations trait handles data access (CRUD) — enforced on all consumers
- Domain logic lives as pure functions on types (Sprint::assignable) — shared but not enforced
- TuiContext/CliContext fields like sprints: Vec<Sprint> are pub, so consumers can hand-roll their own filters

## Research Needed

Investigate making domain data private on context types and only exposing it through methods that enforce business rules. Questions to answer:

- Which fields/operations have business rules that consumers must conform to?
- What would the API surface look like if fields were private?
- How much of the codebase directly accesses ctx.sprints, ctx.cards, etc. vs going through methods?
- What is the ripple effect of making these fields private?
- Is there a middle ground (e.g. private for writes, pub for reads)?

## Goal

Ensure all consumers (TUI, CLI, MCP, future API) cannot accidentally bypass domain business rules, without over-engineering the abstraction layer.
cards.title: Research enforcing domain business rules via private accessors

cards.description:
## Problem

The current workflow bumps the version on every merge to develop. This causes:

1. One version bump per feature/fix/refactor instead of one per release
2. Risk of mismatch between the bumped version in Cargo.toml and the published version on crates.io
3. Bump and publish can get out of sync, delaying the actual publish by one push to master

## Current Flow

1. PR merges to develop → aggregate-changesets.yml bumps version immediately
2. PR from develop to master → release.yml publishes and tags

The gap between steps 1 and 2 is where things go wrong. The version is already bumped but not yet published, so any failure or extra commit can desync the two.

## Desired Flow

1. PRs merge to develop — changesets accumulate, NO version bump
2. When ready to release, create PR from develop to master
3. On merge to master: evaluate all accumulated changesets, determine aggregate bump type, bump version, update changelog, publish to crates.io, create tag — all atomically
4. If any step fails, the whole thing rolls back (no partial state)

## Research Needed

- How to make bump + changelog + publish + tag atomic in a single workflow
- What happens if crates.io publish fails mid-way (some crates published, some not)
- Whether to keep the CI changeset validation on develop PRs (probably yes)
- How to handle the develop → master sync after a release without conflicts
- Review scripts/bump-version.sh, scripts/publish-crates.sh, and both workflows for the refactor

## Risk

The previous workflow was designed to avoid exactly the sync issues we're now hitting. Need to be careful not to introduce new failure modes while fixing the current ones.
cards.title: Redesign release workflow: defer version bump to master merge

## Gap

The `KanbanOperations` trait (`crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs`) defines 3 bulk card operations:
- `bulk_archive_cards` (line 62)
- `bulk_move_cards` (line 63)
- `bulk_assign_sprint` (line 64)

But `bulk_restore_cards` and `bulk_delete_cards` are missing entirely — no trait method, no CLI handler, no TUI handler, no MCP tool. The single-item `restore_card` and `delete_card` are fully implemented across all layers.

## Implementation

### 1. Trait — `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs`
Add two methods alongside the existing bulk ops (after line 64):
```rust
fn bulk_restore_cards(&mut self, ids: Vec<Uuid>, column_id: Option<Uuid>) -> KanbanResult<usize>;
fn bulk_delete_cards(&mut self, ids: Vec<Uuid>) -> KanbanResult<usize>;
```

### 2. CLI context — `crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs`
Implement both methods following the `bulk_archive_cards` pattern (lines 494-501): loop over ids, call single-item method, count successes.

### 3. CLI handler — `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs`
Add `BulkRestore` and `BulkDelete` variants to the card command enum and wire handler functions (parse `--ids` CSV, call context method, print result).

### 4. TUI context — `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs`
Same loop-and-count pattern as CLI context (mirror `bulk_archive_cards` at lines 391-398).

### 5. MCP context — `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs`
Delegate to CLI via `execute_with_retry`, following `bulk_archive_cards` pattern (lines 392-402):
- `bulk_restore_cards` → `["card", "bulk-restore", "--ids", &ids_csv]`
- `bulk_delete_cards` → `["card", "bulk-delete", "--ids", &ids_csv]`

### 6. MCP tools — `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs`
Add `tool_bulk_restore_cards` and `tool_bulk_delete_cards` alongside existing bulk tools (lines 828-860), using the `spawn_op!` macro.
cards.title: Add bulk_restore and bulk_delete to KanbanOperations trait

cards.description:
**From PR #165 review**

The same `parse_datetime` function exists in two places:
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:87-102`
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/sprint.rs:5-18`

These are nearly identical implementations that parse both RFC 3339 and YYYY-MM-DD date formats. Should be extracted to a shared utility in `kanban-domain` or `kanban-core` to avoid drift between the two implementations.
cards.title: Extract duplicated parse_datetime into shared utility

cards.description:
**From PR #165 review**

In `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs:75-81`, `add_field_str` only handles `FieldUpdate::Set`. When `FieldUpdate::Clear` is passed, it silently does nothing.

This is currently correct for sprint prefix/card_prefix (which use separate `--clear-*` flags handled elsewhere), but the method name doesn't signal this limitation. If someone uses `add_field_str` for a field that needs clear support without adding explicit handling, it will silently drop the clear intent.

Consider either:
- Renaming to make the limitation explicit
- Adding a `clear_flag` parameter to handle the Clear variant
- Adding a doc comment warning about the behavior
cards.title: ArgsBuilder.add_field_str silently ignores FieldUpdate::Clear

cards.description:
**From PR #165 review**

In `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs:276-310`, `update_card` handles `FieldUpdate::Clear` for `due_date` (line 303-305) but NOT for `description` (line 281-283) or `points` (line 294-296).

If `FieldUpdate::Clear` is passed for description or points, it silently does nothing — the CLI args won't include a `--clear-description` or `--clear-points` flag.

The PR intentionally removed broken `clear_description`/`clear_points` from the MCP request schema (UpdateCardRequest), which is correct. But the `McpContext` implementation of `KanbanOperations` should still handle the trait contract correctly if called programmatically (e.g., from tests or future code).

Needs corresponding `--clear-description` and `--clear-points` CLI flags to be added first, then the context can handle them.
cards.title: McpContext.update_card doesn't handle Clear for description/points
SyncExecutor error classification relies on fragile string matching

cards.description:
**From PR #165 review**

In `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs:593`:
```rust
req.wip_limit.map(|w| FieldUpdate::Set(w as i32))
```

And similarly in `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/column.rs:52`:
```rust
args.wip_limit.map(|w| w as i32)
```

Both use `as i32` which is a potentially lossy cast. If `wip_limit > i32::MAX` (2,147,483,647), it wraps to a negative value. While unlikely in practice (WIP limits are typically single digits), the schema accepts `u32` so technically values up to 4 billion are valid input.

Consider using `TryFrom` with an error, or capping at a reasonable maximum (e.g., 1000), or changing the domain type to `u32` to match.
cards.title: Lossy u32-to-i32 cast for wip_limit in MCP and CLI handlers

cards.description:
**From PR #165 review**

The integration tests in `crates/kanban-mcp/tests/integration.rs` cover happy paths thoroughly (13 tests for board CRUD, column CRUD, card lifecycle, sprint lifecycle, bulk ops, export/import). However, there are no tests for:

- **Invalid UUID handling** — verify parse_uuid returns proper MCP error
- **Conflict retries** — verify execute_with_retry actually retries on ConflictDetected
- **Timeout behavior** — verify the 30s timeout fires and returns the right error
- **Operations on nonexistent entities** — only `board_get_nonexistent` is tested; missing column_get_nonexistent, card_get_nonexistent, sprint_get_nonexistent
- **Delete of nonexistent entity** — verify proper error propagation
- **Bulk operations with invalid IDs** — partial failure behavior

Unit tests cover parser edge cases well (32 tests), but the integration layer needs error path coverage to prevent regressions.

cards.description:
## Problem

TUI can update all fields on BoardUpdate (10), CardUpdate (11), and SprintUpdate (7), but CLI and MCP can only update a subset:

- **BoardUpdate**: 4/10 fields exposed (missing: task_sort_field, task_sort_order, sprint_duration_days, task_list_view, active_sprint_id, completion_column_id)
- **CardUpdate**: 6/11 fields exposed (missing: position, column_id, sprint_id, assigned_prefix, card_prefix)
- **SprintUpdate**: 5/7 fields exposed (missing: name_index, status)

Some missing fields have dedicated methods (move_card, assign_card_to_sprint, activate/complete/cancel_sprint), so those are intentional. But `assigned_prefix`, `card_prefix` on cards and all the board settings have NO way to be set outside TUI.

## Root Cause

Architectural asymmetry:

1. **TUI** (`tui_context.rs`) operates directly on in-memory Rust structs. It constructs full `CardUpdate`/`BoardUpdate`/`SprintUpdate` structs and passes them to the command executor. No serialization boundary.

2. **CLI** (`cli.rs` + handlers) parses command-line arguments via clap, then constructs the update structs from those args. If no clap flag exists for a field, it's unreachable.

3. **MCP** (`context.rs`) builds CLI argument vectors and shells out to the kanban binary, parsing JSON responses. It inherits all CLI limitations.

The domain layer supports all fields. The bottleneck is entirely in CLI argument definitions.

## Why This Happened

The KanbanOperations trait enforces method parity (all 37 operations exist everywhere), but it doesn't enforce field-level completeness within those methods. The trait signature is:

```rust
fn update_card(&mut self, id: Uuid, updates: CardUpdate) -> Result<Card>;
```

Both TUI and CLI implement this, but TUI populates all CardUpdate fields while CLI only populates fields that have corresponding clap arguments.

## Solution Options

### Option A: Add missing CLI flags
Add clap arguments for all missing fields in the update commands. Straightforward but expands CLI surface area significantly.

Files to modify:
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs` - Add clap args
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs` - Wire args to CardUpdate
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/board.rs` - Wire args to BoardUpdate
- MCP would automatically get parity since it delegates to CLI

### Option B: Direct domain access for MCP
Have MCP implement KanbanOperations by directly manipulating the data file (like TUI does) instead of shelling out to CLI. This would give MCP full field access without CLI changes.

Downside: Duplicates file I/O logic that currently lives in CLI.

### Option C: Extract shared library
Factor out the file I/O and state management into a shared library that both TUI and a new "direct" MCP implementation can use. CLI remains a thin wrapper.

This is the cleanest long-term but highest effort.

## Recommendation

Option A is pragmatic for now. The missing fields are:
- Card: `assigned_prefix`, `card_prefix` (2 fields)
- Board: `task_sort_field`, `task_sort_order`, `sprint_duration_days`, `task_list_view`, `active_sprint_id`, `completion_column_id` (6 fields)

Card prefixes are useful for CLI users. Board settings are more TUI-specific but could still be exposed.

## Files Reference

- Trait: `crates/kanban-domain/src/operations.rs:19-89`
- TUI impl: `crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs:92-571`
- CLI impl: `crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs:172-685`
- MCP impl: `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs:121-554`
- CLI args: `crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs`
cards.title: CLI/MCP field parity gap caused by architectural asymmetry

# Create and Deploy Kanban Project Landing Page

## Summary
Designed and implemented a landing page for the kanban terminal-based project management tool at kanban.yoon.se. The page showcases the   
project's capabilities, features, and installation methods using a terminal-inspired design that matches the yoon.se design system.       

## What Was Done

### 1. Landing Page Design & Content
- Created semantic HTML5 structure with 5 main sections:
  - **Hero**: Project title, tagline, demo GIF, installation CTAs (cargo + nix)
  - **Features**: 6 feature cards highlighting vim-like power, speed, three interfaces, task management, developer workflow, and local-first approach
  - **Installation**: Three installation methods (crates.io, nix, from source) with quick start guide
  - **Roadmap**: v0.2.0 status with completed/in-progress/planned items
  - **Footer**: Links to GitHub, Crates.io, docs, and author/license info

### 2. Design System Integration
- Matched yoon.se terminal-inspired dark theme:
  - Colors: GitHub Dark (#0d1117 bg, #c9714a accent)
  - Typography: SF Mono for headings, system sans for body
  - Spacing: 8-point grid system
  - No border-radius (sharp terminal aesthetic)
- Terminal-style components:
  - `$ ` prefix for install commands in accent color
  - `> ` prefix for links (yoon design pattern)
  - Monospace headings and code blocks
- Fully responsive: desktop (3-col), tablet (2-col), mobile (1-col)

### 3. Static Site Implementation
- **index.html**: 158 lines, semantic HTML with proper meta tags
- **styles.css**: 576 lines, complete design system with CSS variables and responsive layout
- **demo.gif**: 997KB animated TUI demo (copied from project root)
- No external dependencies, fast loading, SEO-friendly

### 4. Nix Build Integration
- Created `web/default.nix` for reproducible builds
- Added `kanban-web` package to main `flake.nix`
- Build command: `nix build .#kanban-web`
- Output: Static files ready for deployment

## Files Created
- `/Users/max/memes/kanban/web/index.html`
- `/Users/max/memes/kanban/web/styles.css`
- `/Users/max/memes/kanban/web/demo.gif`
- `/Users/max/memes/kanban/web/default.nix`

## Deployment
Ready for deployment to kanban.yoon.se:

```bash
nix build .#kanban-web
cp -r result/* /var/www/kanban.yoon.se/
```

Success Criteria

✅ Landing page matches yoon.se design system (dark theme, terminal aesthetic)
✅ All 5 content sections implemented with accurate project information
✅ Responsive design works across desktop, tablet, and mobile
✅ Demo GIF displays correctly and animates
✅ Installation commands (cargo + nix) clearly presented
✅ Nix build support added for reproducible deployment
✅ Fast loading with minimal file size (20KB HTML+CSS)
✅ SEO-optimized with proper meta tags and semantic HTML
✅ Follows yoon BRAND_GUIDE principles (direct, humble, technical, authentic)
✅ Files committed to git with proper commit message

This captures the full scope of work completed for the landing page project!

## Implementation

Wires the existing CardIdentifierSearcher (from KAN-197) through to MCP tools, CLI commands, and the KanbanOperations trait so that 'KAN-5', 'kan-5', or '5' can be used anywhere a card UUID is currently required.

## Changes

### kanban-domain: KanbanOperations trait
- Added `find_card_by_identifier(&self, identifier: &str) -> KanbanResult<Option<Card>>` to the trait

### kanban-cli / kanban-tui / kanban-mcp: Context implementations
- CliContext and TuiContext: implemented via CardIdentifierSearcher::new + matches()
- McpContext: delegates to `card find-by-identifier <identifier>` CLI subcommand

### kanban-cli: CLI changes
- Changed `id: Uuid` -> `id: String` in all single-card CardAction variants (Get, Update, Move, Archive, Restore, Delete, AssignSprint, UnassignSprint, BranchName, GitCheckout)
- Added `FindByIdentifier { identifier: String }` subcommand (`card find-by-identifier`)
- Added `resolve_card_id` helper: tries Uuid::parse_str, falls back to find_card_by_identifier

### kanban-mcp: MCP tool changes
- Added async `resolve_card_id` helper (UUID parse -> spawn_blocking find_card_by_identifier)
- Replaced parse_uuid(&req.card_id) with resolve_card_id(&self.ctx, &req.card_id).await? in all 10 card tools: get, update, move, archive, restore, delete, assign_sprint, unassign_sprint, branch_name, git_checkout
- Updated schemars descriptions for all card_id fields to mention identifier support

## Commits
- feat(domain): add find_card_by_identifier to KanbanOperations trait
- feat(cli,tui,mcp): implement find_card_by_identifier in all contexts
- feat(cli): accept card identifier (e.g. KAN-5) in all card commands
- feat(mcp): resolve card identifier (e.g. KAN-5) in all card tools
cards.title: Find cards by prefix+increment identifier (e.g. KAN-5)

cards.description:
find_card_by_identifier uses .find() and returns the first match, which is ambiguous when two cards on different boards share the same card number and prefix (e.g. two boards both using KAN prefix both having card #210).

## Problem

CardIdentifierSearcher::matches() checks prefix+number but has no board scope. If the caller does not filter by board first, the wrong card can be returned — as demonstrated when 'KAN-210' resolved to an older card instead of the newly created one.

## Options

- Add an optional board_id scope to find_card_by_identifier so callers can narrow the search
- In CLI/MCP contexts where a single board is in use, auto-scope to that board
- Return all matches and error if ambiguous (forcing the caller to use a UUID)
- Document the limitation and require UUID when identifier is ambiguous
cards.title: Disambiguate card identifier lookup across boards
