## Goal
Reduce token consumption when listing cards via MCP and CLI by making descriptions opt-in and adding pagination.

## Problem
- MCP `list_cards` returns full `Card` objects including description and sprint_logs by default — very verbose
- No pagination means all cards are returned at once, which can be huge on large boards

## Plan

### 1. MCP: default to `CardSummary` in `list_cards` (`kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs`)
- Change return type from `Vec<Card>` to `Vec<CardSummary>` by default
- Add `include_description: Option<bool>` to `ListCardsRequest` — when true, return full `Card` objects instead
- Update tool description to document this behaviour

### 2. CLI: add `--description` flag to `card list` (`kanban-cli/src/cli.rs` + `handlers/card.rs`)
- CLI already returns `CardSummary` by default (good)
- Add `#[arg(long)] pub description: bool` to `CardListArgs`
- When `--description` is set, collect `Vec<Card>` instead of `Vec<CardSummary>`

### 3. Pagination for both MCP and CLI
- Add `page: Option<u32>` and `page_size: Option<u32>` to `ListCardsRequest` (MCP) and `CardListArgs` (CLI)
- Default `page_size`: 50
- Apply pagination after filtering in the handler layer (no domain changes needed)
- Return pagination metadata in response: `total`, `page`, `page_size`, `total_pages`

## Files Changed
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` — `ListCardsRequest` fields + handler logic
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs` — `CardListArgs` fields
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs` — pagination + description opt-in logic
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/output.rs` — paginated list response type (if needed)
cards.title: Add description opt-in and pagination to card listing

cards.description:
## Context
Follows from KAN-212 which adds `CardSummary` as the default list response and introduces `PaginatedList<T>` as a shared response wrapper for MCP and CLI.

## Goal
Extend those primitives into the TUI so the entire stack shares the same lean data model for card listing.

## Design Discussion

### Current TUI behaviour
- Loads full `Card` objects into memory for the list view (includes description, sprint_logs)
- Manages its own viewport/scroll pagination via `Page` in `kanban-core/src/pagination.rs`
- `Page` is viewport-aware: tracks `scroll_offset` + `viewport_height` → produces `visible_indices` for rendering, with above/below scroll indicators

### Proposed architecture
- TUI card list view uses `CardSummary` instead of `Card` (no description or sprint_logs in memory)
- Full `Card` is fetched on demand only when opening the card detail/edit panel
- `PaginatedList<T>` (introduced in KAN-212) becomes the fetch/response layer
- The existing `Page` scroll logic continues to own viewport slicing — it sits on top of `PaginatedList<CardSummary>` as the data source rather than a raw `Vec<Card>`

### Layering
```
PaginatedList<CardSummary>   ← data fetch / response layer (shared with CLI + MCP)
         ↓
      Page / PageInfo         ← scroll + viewport logic (TUI-only, unchanged)
         ↓
   render_strategy.rs         ← rendering (unchanged)
```

### Key distinction
`PaginatedList` is a static response wrapper (total, page, page_size, items).
`Page` is a dynamic scroll state that reacts to viewport resize and key events.
They are complementary, not replacements for each other.

## Scope

### Changes required
- `kanban-tui`: change card list state from `Vec<Card>` to `Vec<CardSummary>`
- `kanban-tui`: fetch full `Card` only on detail panel open (lazy load)
- `kanban-tui`: ensure all list-rendering paths use `CardSummary` fields only
- `kanban-tui`: card detail panel continues to use full `Card`
- Verify no description/sprint_log fields are accessed during list rendering

### Out of scope
- Changing `Page` / `PageInfo` scroll logic (leave untouched)
- Server-side pagination for the TUI (TUI still loads all summaries into memory and slices locally — network/IO is not the bottleneck here)
cards.title: Refactor TUI card list to use CardSummary and PaginatedList

cards.description:
## Goal
Eliminate the read-modify-write cycle when editing card descriptions via CLI and MCP. Currently every edit requires reading the full description, reconstructing it client-side, and writing it back as a full replacement. This is expensive for humans and wastes tokens for LLMs.

## Problem
`FieldUpdate<T>` only supports `Set(T)`, `Clear`, and `NoChange`. Every description edit goes through `update_card` with a full replacement. For an LLM editing one section of a structured markdown description this means:
1. Call `get_card` to read current description (~N tokens)
2. Mentally reconstruct the full text with the desired change
3. Call `update_card` with the entire description rewritten (~N tokens again)

## Proposed Operations

### 1. Append
Add text to the end of the description.
```
CLI:  --append-description "text"
MCP:  append_description: "text"
```
Inserts a newline separator if the current description is non-empty.

### 2. Prepend
Add text to the beginning of the description.
```
CLI:  --prepend-description "text"
MCP:  prepend_description: "text"
```
Inserts a newline separator if the current description is non-empty.

### 3. Find & Replace
Replace a specific substring within the description.
```
CLI:  --find "old text" --replace "new text"
MCP:  find: "old text", replace: "new text"
```
Replaces the first occurrence only. Error if `find` string is not found (avoids silent no-ops).
Optional `replace_all: bool` flag to replace all occurrences.

### 4. Section Replace (markdown-aware)
Replace the content under a specific `##` heading without touching other sections.
```
CLI:  --section "Plan" --section-content "new content"
MCP:  section: "Plan", section_content: "new content"
```
Matches headings case-insensitively. The section spans from the matched heading to the next same-or-higher-level heading (or end of string). Replaces only the body, preserving the heading line itself.

Example — given description:
```markdown
## Goal
Reduce tokens.

## Plan
Old plan text.

## Notes
Some notes.
```
`section: "Plan", section_content: "New plan text."` produces:
```markdown
## Goal
Reduce tokens.

## Plan
New plan text.

## Notes
Some notes.
```

Error if the section heading is not found.

### 5. Section Append
Append text within a specific section rather than at the end of the whole description.
```
CLI:  --section "Notes" --append-section "text"
MCP:  section: "Notes", append_section: "text"
```

## Domain Layer Design

### Option A — extend `FieldUpdate<T>`
Add `Append(String)` and `Prepend(String)` variants. Constrained to `T = String` at the call sites.
Downside: `FieldUpdate` is generic and these variants only make sense for strings — slightly leaky abstraction.

### Option B — dedicated `DescriptionUpdate` enum (preferred)
Replace `FieldUpdate<String>` for the description field in `CardUpdate` with a purpose-built enum:
```rust
pub enum DescriptionUpdate {
    NoChange,
    Set(String),
    Clear,
    Append(String),
    Prepend(String),
    FindReplace { find: String, replace: String, replace_all: bool },
    ReplaceSection { heading: String, content: String },
    AppendSection { heading: String, content: String },
}
```
Apply logic lives on `Card` as `fn apply_description_update(&mut self, update: DescriptionUpdate) -> KanbanResult<()>`.
Returns `Err` for find-not-found and section-not-found cases.

## CLI Design
All new flags apply only to `card update`. Conflicts:
- `--description` (full replace) is mutually exclusive with all partial-edit flags
- `--find` requires `--replace`
- `--section` requires either `--section-content` or `--append-section`

## MCP Design
`UpdateCardRequest` gains the new optional fields. The handler applies them in order if multiple are set (though passing multiple edit ops in one call should be discouraged in the tool description).
Tool description should explicitly note: "use append_description or section/section_content instead of description when making partial edits — avoids the read-modify-write cycle."

## Files Changed
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/card.rs` — `apply_description_update()` method, section parsing logic
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/field_update.rs` — add `DescriptionUpdate` enum (or extend `FieldUpdate`)
- `crates/kanban-domain/src/lib.rs` — export `DescriptionUpdate`
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs` — new flags on `CardUpdateArgs`
- `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/card.rs` — map flags to `DescriptionUpdate`
- `crates/kanban-mcp/src/lib.rs` — new fields on `UpdateCardRequest`, handler logic

## Notes
- Section parsing should handle both `##` and `###` headings (match heading level exactly, not just prefix)
- Newline normalisation: strip trailing whitespace before appending separators
- All partial-edit operations should set `updated_at` as usual
- Consider whether `find_replace` and `section_*` ops deserve their own MCP tools rather than being fields on `update_card` — cleaner schema but more tools to discover
cards.title: Rich description editing operations for CLI and MCP

cards.description:
---
  Investigation: -V flag shows commit: unknown in release builds

  Root Cause

  The version string is constructed at compile time in crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs:4-8:

  const VERSION: &str = concat!(
      env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
      "\ncommit: ",
      env!("GIT_COMMIT_HASH")
  );

  The commit hash is resolved in crates/kanban-cli/build.rs:8-27 with this priority:

  1. GIT_COMMIT_HASH env var (if set and not empty / not "unknown")
  2. git rev-parse HEAD — works when .git is present
  3. Falls back to "unknown"

  Why It Fails in Release / Nix Builds

  default.nix:14 uses lib.cleanSource ./. which strips the .git directory before building — standard Nix practice for reproducible builds. Without .git, step 2 fails and the fallback fires.

  Neither the Nix derivation (default.nix, flake.nix), the dev shell (shell.nix), nor the CI workflows (ci.yml, release.yml) set GIT_COMMIT_HASH as an env var at build time.

  Why cargo run -- -V Works

  Running directly from the repo means .git exists on disk, so git rev-parse HEAD succeeds in the build script.

  Proposed Fix

  Two separate concerns:

  1. Drop commit line when unknown — Change the version string construction so commit: unknown is never shown. This requires either:
  - A build-time cfg flag (not possible with env! alone)
  - Splitting the version into base + optional suffix evaluated at runtime via clap's .version() method with a String rather than a compile-time &str

  2. Inject hash during Nix/release builds — In default.nix, pass the commit rev as an env var before the build:
  preBuild = ''
    export GIT_COMMIT_HASH="${self.rev or "unknown"}"
  '';
  self.rev is available in flake context when the working tree is clean and committed. The or "unknown" handles dirty trees gracefully.

  Files Involved

  ┌───────────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │               File                │                 Relevance                  │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ crates/kanban-cli/src/cli.rs:4-13 │ Version string constant + clap wiring      │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ crates/kanban-cli/build.rs:1-28   │ Build script that resolves GIT_COMMIT_HASH │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ default.nix:14                    │ lib.cleanSource strips .git                │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ flake.nix                         │ Has access to self.rev for injecting hash  │
  ├───────────────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ .github/workflows/release.yml     │ Does not set GIT_COMMIT_HASH               │
  └───────────────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  Acceptance Criteria

  - kanban -V on a release/Nix build shows only the semver (e.g. 0.2.0), no commit: line
  - kanban -V on a dev build (cargo run) still shows the commit hash
  - kanban -V on a Nix build from a clean committed tree shows the correct short commit hash
  - No commit: unknown output in any scenario
cards.title: version flag

cards.description:
`aggregate-changelog.sh` produces a flat list of every commit message, losing the
per-feature grouping that older entries (≤ 0.1.10) had. The 0.3.0 entry has 60+
raw commit lines instead of sections grouped by ticket/changeset.

**Current output (0.3.0):**
```
- feat: update MCP server tools for full CLI parity
- feat: bring MCP context to full parity with CLI
- fix: error handling in MCP executor
...
```

**Expected output (matching 0.1.10 style):**
```
### KAN-193 Bring MCP to full feature parity (2026-03-17)
- feat: update MCP server tools for full CLI parity
- feat: bring MCP context to full parity with CLI
...

### KAN-212 Paginated list responses (2026-03-17)
- feat(core): add PaginatedList<T> ...
```

## Cause

`aggregate-changelog.sh` reads changeset files (one per ticket) but concatenates
their descriptions into a single flat section rather than emitting a heading per
changeset.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each changeset file produces its own `###` heading in the CHANGELOG, using the
ticket ID and description from the changeset frontmatter
- Commit-level bullet points appear under their respective heading
- The 0.3.0 entry is retroactively corrected to match the grouped format
- Future releases produce grouped output automatically
cards.title: CHANGELOG.md grouping by card

cards.description:
## Bug

`tool_list_cards` in the MCP server always returns at most 50 cards regardless of the actual total, and `total_pages` is always 1.

## Root Cause

The MCP context's `list_cards` delegates to the CLI `card list` command via `execute_list`. The CLI command is called without `--page` or `--page-size` args, so it defaults to page 1 with page_size 50. Only those 50 items are returned to the MCP layer, which then paginates them — meaning `total` is always capped at 50 (the page size), not the actual number of cards.

**Location**: `crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs` — `execute_list` (line 115) and `list_cards` (line 255)

```rust
// execute_list only captures items, ignores pagination metadata
fn execute_list<T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned>(&self, args: &[&str]) -> KanbanResult<Vec<T>> {
    let response: ListResponse<T> = self.executor.execute(args)?;
    Ok(response.items)  // only 50 items from page 1
}
```

## Fix

Update `ListResponse` to include `total_pages`, then loop through all pages in `execute_list` to collect the full result set before returning to the MCP layer for client-side pagination.
cards.title: MCP list_cards pagination returns max 50 cards instead of all cards

cards.description:
## Goal

Migrate all list methods in the `KanbanOperations` trait from `Vec<T>` to `PaginatedList<T>`, and add real pagination support to the TUI.

## Scope

**Trait changes** (`kanban-domain/src/operations.rs`):
- `list_boards`, `list_columns`, `list_cards`, `list_sprints`, `list_archived_cards` — change return type to `KanbanResult<PaginatedList<T>>`

**`PaginatedList<T>`** (`kanban-core/src/paginated_list.rs`):
- Add `Deref<Target = Vec<T>>` so existing call sites don't break

**3 context impls** — CliContext, TuiContext, McpContext — update all 5 methods each.

**TUI** (`kanban-tui`):
- TuiContext impls currently return full in-memory vecs — initially wrap as `PaginatedList` with page=1/page_size=total
- Add real TUI pagination UI (scroll through pages, page indicator)

## Notes

- MCP pagination fix (KAN-217) is a prerequisite — it solves the immediate bug with a targeted fix
- Integration tests will need minor updates (`.len()` → `.total`, indexing → `.items[i]`) in places where Deref doesn't cover

## Prerequisite consideration

`KanbanContext` fields (`boards`, `columns`, `cards`, etc.) are currently `pub`. When this card lands, evaluate converting to `pub(crate)` or accessor methods to enforce the command pattern — direct field mutation bypasses the command log and will conflict with transactional semantics in the SQLite backend (KAN-159).

cards.description:
## Challenge
When the terminal window was too small to show all keybindings in the help popup, entries were silently cut off with no scroll support.

## Solution
Implemented scrollable help menu by reusing the existing `ListComponent` infrastructure from the card lists, following the same patterns used throughout the rest of the TUI.

## Changes

### `crates/kanban-core/src/pagination.rs`
- Added `Page::get_adjusted_viewport_height(raw)` — computes content rows after reserving lines for scroll indicators (above=1 if scrolled, below=1 if items extend past viewport).

### `crates/kanban-tui/src/components/generic_list.rs`
- `ListComponent::get_adjusted_viewport_height` now delegates to `Page::get_adjusted_viewport_height` (deduplication).

### `crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs`
- Replaced `help_selection: SelectionState` + `help_page: Page` with `help_list: ListComponent`.
- On help open: `help_list.update_item_count(bindings.len())` + `set_scroll_offset(0)`.
- On help close (3 locations): `help_list.update_item_count(0)`.
- Navigation (j/k): uses two-step `ensure_selected_visible` pattern matching `navigation_handlers.rs`.
- Key-shortcut jump: uses `help_list.jump_to(index)` + `ensure_selected_visible`.

### `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs` — `render_help_popup`
- Layout changed to `[Length(2), Min(0), Length(2)]`: fixed header, scrollable bindings body, fixed footer.
- `all_lines` now contains only the N binding lines (no header/footer offset arithmetic needed).
- Uses `help_list.get_adjusted_viewport_height` + `help_list.get_render_info` for correct scroll state.
- Scroll indicators rendered via shared `render_scroll_indicators` helper with label `"item"` (fixes "entrys" pluralisation bug from `"entry"`).
- Fixed footer (hint line) always visible regardless of scroll position.

## Commits
- `feat(pagination): add get_adjusted_viewport_height to Page`
- `refactor(generic_list): delegate get_adjusted_viewport_height to Page`
- `feat(help): replace help_selection+help_page with help_list ListComponent`
- `feat(help): fixed header/footer layout with ListComponent scroll in render_help_popup`

2026-03-21 00:20:17
---

PR #185 — KAN-221: Scrollable Help Menu

Overview

Replaces the static Paragraph-based help popup with a ListComponent-backed scrollable list. Fixes
clipping on small terminals, pins the header/footer, and fixes a pluralisation bug ("entrys" →
"items"). Also generalises render_scroll_indicators and extracts get_adjusted_viewport_height up to
 kanban-core.

---
Code Quality

Positives:
- The get_adjusted_viewport_height extraction to Page is a clean improvement — ListComponent now
just delegates, eliminating duplication
- render_scroll_indicators generalisation is well done; the split-call pattern (above and below
separately) is a bit awkward but avoids a larger API change
- render_help_popup signature change to &mut App is appropriate given it now writes
help_viewport_height
- The two-step ensure_selected_visible pattern for navigation is consistent with the existing
navigation_handlers.rs approach

Issues:

1. help_viewport_height as mutable state on App is a design smell. Viewport height is a render-time
 concern, not application state. It's written during render_help_popup and read during event
handling (handle_help_keys). This creates a frame-lag: on the very first keypress after opening
help, help_viewport_height is 0 (initialised in open_help via set_scroll_offset(0) but never set).
The adjusted height will be wrong until the first render frame completes.

// app.rs:513
self.help_list.update_item_count(context.bindings.len());
self.help_list.set_scroll_offset(0);
// help_viewport_height is still 0 here
2. render_scroll_indicators called twice per render site for above/below. The split above/below
calls work but produce a confusing API — a caller passing show_above=false, items_above=0 just to
get the below indicator feels wrong. A struct or two-field tuple would be cleaner, or the function
could take an Option for each side. Minor, but it was touched in this PR so it's worth noting.
3. update_item_count(0) used for "reset" (lines 870, 884, 1707). The intent is clearing the list,
but zeroing total_items is semantically different from "close/reset". If ListComponent ever gains
invariants around total_items, this could silently break. A reset() method would be clearer.
4. render_help_popup signature is now &mut App but it's called from the render path. Render
functions mutating application state is an architectural concern — it makes render_* functions
harder to reason about and test. The help_viewport_height field is the only reason for this; see
point 1.
5. all_lines is built eagerly (iterating all bindings), then only page_info.visible_indices are
rendered. For typical keybinding counts this is fine, but it's unnecessary work — the visible lines
 could be built directly from visible_indices.

---
Potential Issues / Risks

- Frame-lag on first navigation: If the user opens help and immediately presses j before the first
render, help_viewport_height == 0 means get_adjusted_viewport_height(0) == 0, so
ensure_selected_visible(0) is a no-op. The selection will scroll correctly after the next render.
Low severity but worth a comment.
- render_help_popup not called if help mode is never rendered (unlikely in practice, but
theoretically help_viewport_height stays 0 if render is skipped for any reason).
- No test coverage added for the new scroll logic in the help context — the existing pattern in the
 codebase seems to rely on manual testing, which is noted in the PR description.

---
Suggestions

1. Pass viewport_height into handle_help_keys from the call site, or compute it from a
terminal-size accessor, rather than caching it on App.
2. Consider a ListComponent::reset() method instead of update_item_count(0).
3. Add a render_scroll_indicators_pair variant or use a small struct to avoid the split above/below
 calls.
4. Build rendered_lines directly from page_info.visible_indices to avoid the intermediate all_lines
 allocation.

---
Verdict

The core fix is correct and the approach is sound. The main concern is help_viewport_height on App
creating a render/event coupling — it works in practice due to the TUI render loop but is fragile.
All other issues are minor. Approve with nits — the frame-lag concern is worth addressing before
merge if there's appetite, otherwise it should at minimum be documented.

Follow-up fixes from KAN-123 search improvements:
- `gg` didn't scroll view to top — added `ensure_selected_visible` to `handle_jump_to_top`
- Unicode panic risk in `build_title_spans` — byte offsets from `title_lower` could differ from `title` for chars that expand on lowercasing (e.g. Turkish İ)
- `search_query` expression repeated 3x in `render_strategy.rs` — added `SearchState::active_query()` helper
- Active-search footer only showed `ESC: clear search` — updated to `j/k: navigate | ESC: clear`
- `n`/`N` were wired to navigate when search active — removed, `n` is new card only

## Summary

Deep-dive audit across architecture, code reuse, abstractions, test structure, file sizes, functional composition, and overall quality. Prioritized by velocity impact (what unlocks faster development first).

---

## Priority 1 — App God Object
**File**: `crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs` (2,060 lines)
**Velocity Impact**: Critical — every feature touches this file, causing constant merge conflicts

The `App` struct has 58 public fields and 37+ methods spanning: UI state, dialog modes, selection tracking, animation, search, filters, sorting, relationships, sprint assignment, file watching, and save workers. All handler files are just `impl App` extensions.

**Fix**: Split into focused sub-structs: ModalState, SelectionState, FilterState, MultiSelectState, AnimationState, RelationshipState, DialogState.

**Effort**: 4–6 days

[x] 2026-04-06 22:53:41
---

## Priority 2 — Dialog System Consolidation
**Files**: `handlers/dialog_handlers.rs`, `handlers/popup_handlers.rs`, `app.rs`, `ui.rs`, `DialogMode` enum
**Velocity Impact**: Critical — adding one new dialog requires touching 4+ files

`DialogMode` has 20+ variants. Each requires: an enum entry, a match arm in `handle_key_event()`, a render function in `ui.rs`, and a handler somewhere across 3 handler files.

**Fix**: Create a `DialogState` trait. Each dialog becomes a self-contained struct. Adding a dialog = creating one file.

**Effort**: 3–4 days

---

## Priority 3 — Keybinding System
**Files**: `app.rs:336–730`, `keybindings/registry.rs`, `keybindings/*.rs`
**Velocity Impact**: High — keybindings are hardcoded in two places; adding/changing a binding is risky

`KeybindingRegistry` exists and generates help text but is NOT used for event routing. Actual dispatch happens in a giant raw match in `handle_key_event()` (lines 536–730).

**Fix**: Make the registry the single source of truth. Route events through it. Makes keybindings user-configurable.

**Effort**: 2–3 days

[x] 2026-04-06 22:53:34
---

## Priority 4 — `ui.rs` Rendering Monolith Split
**File**: `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs` (1,792 lines)
**Velocity Impact**: High — all render work lands in one file; unrelated UI changes conflict constantly

Contains all render functions for every view, every dialog, panels, footer, help overlay. 20+ dialog render functions all live here.

**Fix**: Split into `ui/views/`, `ui/dialogs/` (one file per dialog), `ui/footer.rs`, `ui/help.rs`, `ui/banner.rs`.

**Effort**: 2–3 days (mostly mechanical file splitting)

[x] 2026-04-06 22:53:58 I think we can call this done right?
---

## Priority 5 — Context Triplication
**Files**: `tui_context.rs` (583L), `kanban-cli/context.rs` (697L), `kanban-mcp/context.rs` (688L)
**Velocity Impact**: High — same `KanbanOperations` impl copy-pasted 3 times; every domain change requires 3 edits

All three contexts implement `KanbanOperations` with nearly identical logic. `JsonFileStore` used directly in all three.

**Fix**: Extract shared `BaseContext` into `kanban-domain` or a new `kanban-context` crate.

**Effort**: 3–4 days

---

## Priority 6 — Test Structure (Shadow `tests/` Directories)
**Velocity Impact**: Medium — hard to find, run, or scope tests; no isolated domain command tests

`kanban-core`, `kanban-domain`, `kanban-persistence` all use inline `#[cfg(test)]`. `mockall` is in workspace but not used with `#[automock]`. Domain command files (`board_commands.rs`, `card_commands.rs`, etc.) have zero isolated unit tests.

**Fix**: Move inline tests to `tests/` directories mirroring `src/`. Add `#[automock]` to persistence traits.

**Effort**: 2–3 days

---

## Priority 7 — Handler Reorganization
**Files**: `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/` (4,979 lines across 6 files)
**Velocity Impact**: Medium — unclear where to add new behavior; handlers too long to scan

- `detail_view_handlers.rs`: 3 functions, 1,056 lines (one fn is 280+ lines)
- `count_headers_in_viewport()` duplicated between `navigation_handlers.rs:24` and `render_strategy.rs:17`
- Column move-up/move-down are 90% identical (~90 lines duplicate logic)
- Dialog match arm pattern repeated 8 times across handler files

**Fix**: Reorganize by domain concern. Extract HOF for repeated dialog pattern. Deduplicate viewport helper.

**Effort**: 2–3 days

---

## Priority 8 — Domain Model Sizes
**Files**: `board.rs` (918L), `card.rs` (728L), `card_lifecycle.rs` (600L), `card_graph.rs` (636L)
**Velocity Impact**: Medium — violates 300-line guideline 2–3x; slow to navigate

**Fix**: Split `board.rs` → `board/mod.rs` + `board/operations.rs` + `board/serialization.rs`. Same for `card.rs`.

**Effort**: 1–2 days

---

## Priority 9 — Type Safety via Newtypes
**Files**: All `type XxxId = Uuid` declarations in domain models
**Velocity Impact**: Medium — current aliases allow silently swapping IDs; bugs are slow to find

`type BoardId = Uuid`, `type CardId = Uuid` etc. mean a fn taking `(card_id: Uuid, column_id: Uuid)` will compile fine if you swap the args.

**Fix**: Convert to newtypes: `pub struct CardId(Uuid)`. Zero runtime cost.

**Effort**: 1–2 days

---

## Priority 10 — Remove Dead Abstractions
**File**: `kanban-core/src/traits.rs` lines 5–20
**Velocity Impact**: Low — but causes confusion

`Repository<T, Id>` and `Service<T, Id>` traits are defined but never implemented anywhere. CLAUDE.md references a `kanban-db` crate that doesn't exist.

**Fix**: Delete unused traits, update CLAUDE.md to reflect actual architecture.

**Effort**: 0.5 days

---

## Summary Table

| # | Area | Velocity Impact | Effort |
|---|------|----------------|--------|
| 1 | App God Object (`app.rs` 2,060L, 58 fields) | Critical | 4–6 days |
| 2 | Dialog System (20+ variants, 4-file touch) | Critical | 3–4 days |
| 3 | Keybinding System (hardcoded in 2 places) | High | 2–3 days |
| 4 | `ui.rs` Split (1,792L monolith) | High | 2–3 days |
| 5 | Context Triplication (3x copy-paste) | High | 3–4 days |
| 6 | Test Structure (inline only, no automock) | Medium | 2–3 days |
| 7 | Handler Reorganization (4,979L, duplication) | Medium | 2–3 days |
| 8 | Domain Model Sizes (board 918L, card 728L) | Medium | 1–2 days |
| 9 | Newtype IDs (type aliases, no type safety) | Medium | 1–2 days |
| 10 | Dead Abstractions (Repository/Service traits) | Low | 0.5 days |

**Total**: ~21–31 days. Done in order, each step makes the next faster.

---

Codebase Quality Audit — Prioritized Findings

Scoring Key

- Velocity Impact: How much faster will the team move after this refactor?
- Effort: Low / Medium / High (in dev-days)

---
Priority 1 — App God Object

Files: crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs (2,060 lines)
Velocity Impact: 🔴 Critical — every feature touches this file, causing constant merge conflicts and context overload

The App struct has 58 public fields and 37+ methods spanning: UI state, dialog modes, selection tracking, animation, search, filters,
sorting, relationships, sprint assignment, file watching, and save workers. All handler files (card_handlers.rs, navigation_handlers.rs,
etc.) are just impl App extensions that mutate any of these fields freely.

What to do: Split into focused sub-structs:
```sh
ModalState        { mode, mode_stack }
SelectionState    { board_selection, card_focus, board_focus, focus }
FilterState       { active_sprint_filters, hide_assigned, sort_field, sort_order }
MultiSelectState  { selected_cards, selection_mode_active, sprint_assign_selection }
AnimationState    { animating_cards }
RelationshipState { relationship_card_ids, parents_list, children_list }
DialogState       { input, priority_selection, column_selection, import_files }
```

Effort: 4–6 days | Payoff: Every handler/render file becomes independently navigable. New contributors can own one sub-struct without
reading all 2,060 lines.

---
Priority 2 — Dialog System Consolidation

Files: handlers/dialog_handlers.rs, handlers/popup_handlers.rs, app.rs, ui.rs, DialogMode enum
Velocity Impact: 🔴 Critical — adding one new dialog currently requires touching 4+ files in 4 different places

DialogMode has 20+ variants. Each one requires: an enum entry, a match arm in handle_key_event(), a render function in ui.rs, and a
handler somewhere across 3 handler files. There is no single place to add a dialog.

What to do: Create a DialogState trait:

```rust
pub trait DialogState: Send {
    fn handle_key(&mut self, key: KeyEvent) -> DialogResult;
    fn render(&self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect);
}
```

Each dialog becomes a self-contained struct. Adding a dialog = creating one file.

Effort: 3–4 days | Payoff: Dialogs become plug-and-play. Directly reduces the cost of new features.

---
Priority 3 — Keybinding System

Files: `app.rs:336–730`, `keybindings/registry.rs`, `keybindings/*.rs`
Velocity Impact: 🟠 High — keybindings are hardcoded in two places; adding/changing a binding is risky

The `KeybindingRegistry` exists and generates help text — but is not used for event routing. Actual dispatch happens in a giant raw match in
 `handle_key_event()` (lines 536–730). Key detection uses a fragile string-parsing approach (`keycode_matches_binding_key()`, lines 336–391).

What to do: Make the registry the single source of truth. Route events through it. This also makes keybindings configurable by users.

Effort: 2–3 days | Payoff: Changing or adding keybindings goes from risky multi-file surgery to a one-liner data change.

---
Priority 4 — `ui.rs` Rendering Monolith Split

Files: `crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs` (1,792 lines)
Velocity Impact: 🟠 High — all render work lands in one file; unrelated UI changes conflict constantly

Contains all render functions for every view, every dialog, panels, footer, help overlay. 20+ dialog render functions all live here.

What to do:

```sh
ui/
├── mod.rs          (entry point only)
├── views/
│   ├── main.rs
│   ├── card_detail.rs
│   ├── board_detail.rs
│   └── sprint_detail.rs
├── dialogs/        (one file per DialogMode)
├── footer.rs
├── help.rs
└── banner.rs
```

Effort: 2–3 days (mostly mechanical file splitting) | Payoff: Parallel UI work without conflicts. Each dialog/view is independently
editable.

---
Priority 5 — Context Triplication

Files: kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs (583 lines), kanban-cli/src/context.rs (697 lines), kanban-mcp/src/context.rs (688 lines)
Velocity Impact: 🟠 High — same KanbanOperations impl copy-pasted 3 times; every domain operation change requires 3 edits

All three contexts implement KanbanOperations with nearly identical logic but divergent concrete types (JsonFileStore used directly in all
 three).

What to do: Extract a shared BaseContext (or KanbanContextImpl) into kanban-domain or a new kanban-context crate. Let each consumer
specialize only what differs (persistence transport, output format).

Effort: 3–4 days | Payoff: Domain-level operation changes propagate automatically to CLI, TUI, and MCP.

---
Priority 6 — Test Structure (Shadow tests/ Directories)

Velocity Impact: 🟡 Medium — currently hard to find, run, or scope tests

What exists:
- `kanban-tui/tests/` — 6 integration test files (good)
- `kanban-cli/tests/` — 1 large integration test file (good)
- `kanban-mcp/tests/` — 1 integration test file (good)
- `kanban-core`, `kanban-domain`, `kanban-persistence` — all tests are inline (`#[cfg(test)]` at bottom of each .rs file)

What's missing: Unit tests for kanban-domain commands (board_commands.rs, card_commands.rs, column_commands.rs, sprint_commands.rs — zero
isolated tests). mockall is in the workspace but not used with #[automock].

What to do: Move domain + persistence inline tests to tests/ directories mirroring src/. Add #[automock] to persistence traits so TUI/CLI
can be tested with in-memory stores.

Effort: 2–3 days | Payoff: Faster test discovery, ability to run domain tests in isolation, unblocks testing handlers without full
persistence setup.

---
Priority 7 — Handler Reorganization

Files: `crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/` (4,979 lines total across 6 files)
Velocity Impact: 🟡 Medium — unclear where to add new behavior; handlers too long to scan

Problems:
- `detail_view_handlers.rs` has 3 functions totaling 1,056 lines (one function is 280+ lines)
- `handle_card_detail_key()` and `handle_board_detail_key()` are 230–280 line match blocks
- `count_headers_in_viewport()` is duplicated between `navigation_handlers.rs:24` and `render_strategy.rs:17`
- Column move-up/move-down are 90% identical (~90 lines of duplicate logic)

What to do: Reorganize by domain concern (handlers/card/, handlers/board/, handlers/navigation/). Extract the 8-repeated dialog match
pattern into a single HOF. Deduplicate count_headers_in_viewport.

Effort: 2–3 days | Payoff: Clear home for new handlers, ~150 lines of duplicate code eliminated.

---
Priority 8 — Domain Model Sizes

Files: `kanban-domain/src/board.rs` (918 lines), `card.rs` (728 lines), `card_lifecycle.rs` (600 lines), `dependencies/card_graph.rs` (636 lines)
Velocity Impact: 🟡 Medium — violates the 300-line guideline 2–3x over; slow to navigate

What to do: Split `board.rs` into `board/mod.rs` + `board/operations.rs` + `board/serialization.rs`. Split `card.rs` into `card/mod.rs` +
`card/update.rs` + `card/lifecycle.rs`.

Effort: 1–2 days | Payoff: Navigation speed, reduced cognitive load when editing one concern.

---
Priority 9 — Type Safety via Newtypes

Files: `kanban-domain/src/board.rs:9`, `card.rs:9`, `column.rs`, etc.
Velocity Impact: 🟡 Medium — current type BoardId = Uuid allows swapping IDs silently; bugs are slow to find

// Current: compile-time silent bug possible
`fn move_card(card_id: Uuid, column_id: Uuid)`

// With newtypes: compile error if swapped
`fn move_card(card_id: CardId, column_id: ColumnId)`

Effort: 1–2 days | Payoff: Eliminates a whole class of runtime bugs. Zero cost at runtime.

---
Priority 10 — Remove Dead Abstractions

Files: `kanban-core/src/traits.rs` (lines 5–20)
Velocity Impact: 🟢 Low — but causes confusion for new contributors

`Repository<T, Id>` and `Service<T, Id>` traits are defined but never implemented. The `CLAUDE.md` documentation still references a kanban-db
crate that doesn't exist.

What to do: Either delete these traits and update `CLAUDE.md`, or implement them (the persistence trait pattern exists in
`kanban-persistence/src/traits.rs` and is real).

Effort: 0.5 days | Payoff: Reduces architectural confusion.

---
Summary Table

┌─────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────┬──────────┐
│  #  │          Area          │                  Key File(s)                   │ Velocity Impact │  Effort  │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 1   │ App God Object         │ app.rs (2,060 lines, 58 fields)                │ 🔴 Critical     │ 4–6 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 2   │ Dialog System          │ DialogMode enum + 4 handler files              │ 🔴 Critical     │ 3–4 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 3   │ Keybinding System      │ app.rs:336–730 + keybindings/                  │ 🟠 High         │ 2–3 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 4   │ ui.rs Split            │ ui.rs (1,792 lines)                            │ 🟠 High         │ 2–3 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 5   │ Context Triplication   │ tui_context, cli_context, mcp_context          │ 🟠 High         │ 3–4 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 6   │ Test Structure         │ kanban-domain, kanban-persistence inline tests │ 🟡 Medium       │ 2–3 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 7   │ Handler Reorganization │ handlers/ (4,979 lines)                        │ 🟡 Medium       │ 2–3 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 8   │ Domain Model Sizes     │ board.rs (918), card.rs (728)                  │ 🟡 Medium       │ 1–2 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 9   │ Newtype IDs            │ All type XxxId = Uuid                          │ 🟡 Medium       │ 1–2 days │
├─────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┼──────────┤
│ 10  │ Dead Abstractions      │ kanban-core/src/traits.rs                      │ 🟢 Low          │ 0.5 days │
└─────┴────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┴──────────┘

Total estimated effort: ~21–31 days. Done in order, each step makes the next one faster because the codebase becomes more navigable as you
 go. Items 1–5 are the core unlock for sustained velocity.
cards.title: Codebase Quality Audit — Architecture & Refactor Opportunities

cards.description:
Plan: Decompose app.rs into Focused Modules

Context

crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs is a 2,060-line god object with 58 public fields spanning unrelated concerns: mode navigation, focus tracking, entity selection, filtering, multi-select, dialog state, sprint views, relationships, animations, persistence, and UI state. All 9 handler files (impl App) freely mutate any field. This makes it impossible to discover where to add/change something without reading the entire file, and causes constant merge conflicts.

The goal is purely structural: split fields and their co-located logic into focused sub-structs in named modules with zero behavioral change. After this refactor, adding a new sprint feature means editing sprint_view.rs. Adding a filter means editing filter.rs. The App struct becomes a thin orchestrator.

Target Structure

crates/kanban-tui/src/app/
├── mod.rs          ← Thin App struct + new() + run() + handle_key_event() + execute_action()
├── mode.rs         ← AppMode, DialogMode, ModeManager (push/pop/open_dialog/is_dialog/get_base_mode)
├── focus.rs        ← Focus, CardFocus, BoardFocus, FocusState
├── selection.rs    ← SelectionHub (board/card/sprint selection + active indices)
├── filter.rs       ← FilterState (sprint filters, hide_assigned, sort field/order, search, filter_dialog)
├── multi_select.rs ← MultiSelectState (selected_cards, selection_mode_active)
├── dialog_input.rs ← DialogInputState (import, priority, column, sprint_assign, task_list_view selections)
├── sprint_view.rs  ← SprintTaskPanel enum, SprintViewState (panel + card lists + components)
├── relationship.rs ← RelationshipState (card_ids, selected, selection, search, parents/children lists)
├── view.rs         ← ViewState (strategy, card_list_component, viewport_height, last_frame_area)
├── animation.rs    ← CardAnimation, ANIMATION_DURATION_MS, AnimationState
├── persistence.rs  ← PersistenceState (save_file, app_config, file_watcher, channels)
└── ui_state.rs     ← UiState (banner, help_list, help_pending_action, card_navigation_history)

Migration Steps (each must compile before proceeding)

Step 1: app.rs → app/mod.rs (mkdir + mv). Files Touched: 0. Effort: 5 min
Step 2: Extract enums to mode.rs, re-export. Files Touched: mod.rs. Effort: 20 min
Step 3: Extract Focus/CardFocus/BoardFocus/SprintTaskPanel to focus.rs, sprint_view.rs. Files Touched: mod.rs. Effort: 15 min
Step 4: Extract CardAnimation + constant to animation.rs. Files Touched: mod.rs. Effort: 10 min
Step 5: Introduce ModeManager with Deref; mode+mode_stack → mode: ModeManager. Files Touched: mod.rs + ~20 write sites in handlers/ui.rs. Effort: 75 min
Step 6: Introduce FocusState; 3 fields → focus: FocusState. Files Touched: 9 handler files, ui.rs, render_strategy.rs, keybindings/registry.rs. Effort: 45 min
Step 7: Introduce SelectionHub; 5 fields → selection: SelectionHub. Files Touched: all 9 handlers, app.rs, ui.rs, state/snapshot.rs. Effort: 75 min
Step 8: Introduce FilterState; 7 fields → filter: FilterState. Files Touched: app.rs, navigation/sprint/card/filter_handlers, state/snapshot.rs. Effort: 40 min
Step 9: Introduce MultiSelectState; 2 fields → multi_select: MultiSelectState. Files Touched: navigation/popup/card_handlers, app.rs. Effort: 20 min
Step 10: Introduce DialogInputState; 6 fields → dialog_input: DialogInputState. Files Touched: popup/card/column/detail_view/board_handlers. Effort: 25 min
Step 11: Introduce SprintViewState; 5 fields → sprint_view: SprintViewState. Files Touched: app.rs, popup/card/detail_view_handlers, ui.rs. Effort: 35 min
Step 12: Introduce RelationshipState; 7 fields → relationship: RelationshipState. Files Touched: navigation/popup/card/detail_view_handlers. Effort: 30 min
Step 13: Introduce ViewState; 4 fields → view: ViewState. Files Touched: app.rs, navigation/card_handlers, ui.rs. Effort: 20 min
Step 14: Introduce AnimationState; 1 field → animation: AnimationState. Files Touched: app.rs, card_handlers. Effort: 20 min
Step 15: Introduce PersistenceState; 6 fields → persistence: PersistenceState. Files Touched: app.rs, state/mod.rs only. Effort: 20 min
Step 16: Introduce UiState; 4 fields → ui: UiState; keep App shims. Files Touched: app.rs, detail_view_handlers. Effort: 20 min
Step 17: Move method bodies into sub-struct impls (populate, apply_sort, sync, switch_strategy, animation tick). Files Touched: app.rs + sprint_view/view/animation modules. Effort: 45 min

Total: ~8 hours

Critical Files

- crates/kanban-tui/src/app.rs → becomes app/mod.rs
- crates/kanban-tui/src/ui.rs — updated in steps 6, 11, 13, 16
- crates/kanban-tui/src/state/snapshot.rs — apply_to_app accesses active_board_index + current_sort_field; updated in steps 7 and 8
- crates/kanban-tui/src/keybindings/registry.rs — reads app.mode, app.focus, app.card_focus, app.board_focus; updated in steps 5 and 6
- crates/kanban-tui/src/handlers/detail_view_handlers.rs — touches the most sub-struct categories; use as integration canary

Verification

After each step: cargo check --package kanban-tui
After all steps: cargo build && cargo test --package kanban-tui && cargo clippy -- -D warnings
cards.title: Decompose app.rs into Focused Modules

cards.description:
Follow-up items from code review of PR #189 (KAN-224/decompose-app-rs-into-focused-modules). Zero blockers — PR was merged as-is.

---

## 1. Rename `SelectionHub` to something clearer

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/selection.rs:4`
**Type:** Naming / readability

```rust
pub struct SelectionHub {
    pub board: SelectionState,
    pub active_board_index: Option<usize>,
    pub active_card_index: Option<usize>,
    pub sprint: SelectionState,
    pub active_sprint_index: Option<usize>,
    pub card_navigation_history: Vec<usize>,
}
```

`Hub` implies it coordinates or dispatches between things (like an event hub or message hub). This struct is just a plain data grouping of selection indices and cursor state. A reader seeing `self.selection.board` would not immediately know what "hub" means here.

**Suggested names:** `AppSelection`, `CursorState`, or simply `SelectionState` (if the `kanban_core::SelectionState` name collision can be resolved via aliasing or renaming the core type).

**Action:** Rename the struct and the `App` field from `selection: SelectionHub` to `selection: AppSelection` (or chosen name). Search all handler files for `self.selection.` — they all need updating but it is a trivial find-replace.

---

## 2. `FocusState` mixes two different lifecycle concerns

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/focus.rs:91-95`
**Type:** Design / cohesion

```rust
pub struct FocusState {
    pub active: Focus,        // toggled on every Tab/hjkl press
    pub card_focus: CardFocus,   // only meaningful inside CardDetail mode
    pub board_focus: BoardFocus, // only meaningful inside BoardDetail mode
}
```

`active` is the global panel focus (Boards vs Cards) and changes constantly during normal navigation. `card_focus` and `board_focus` are sub-panel cursors that are only semantically relevant when the app is in `AppMode::CardDetail` or `AppMode::BoardDetail` respectively — outside those modes their values are stale/irrelevant.

Grouping them together in `FocusState` means a reader of `FocusState` has to mentally track when each field is valid. It also means detail-mode logic and navigation logic both mutate the same struct.

**Option A (preferred):** Move `card_focus: CardFocus` into a `CardDetailState` struct (does not exist yet, could live in a new `card_detail.rs`), and `board_focus: BoardFocus` into a `BoardDetailState`. These would be separate `App` fields only consulted in their respective modes.

**Option B (minimal):** Keep them in `FocusState` but rename to `FocusState.panel` (instead of `active`) to signal it is the panel-level concern, and add comments on `card_focus`/`board_focus` that they are detail-mode sub-cursors.

---

## 3. Drop redundant `#[derive(Default)]` on `PersistenceState`

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/persistence.rs:3,16`
**Type:** Cleanup / consistency

```rust
#[derive(Default)]   // <-- never actually called
pub struct PersistenceState {
    pub save_file: Option<String>,
    pub file_change_rx: Option<...>,
    pub file_watcher: Option<...>,
    pub save_worker_handle: Option<...>,
    pub save_completion_rx: Option<...>,
}

impl PersistenceState {
    pub fn new(save_file: Option<String>, save_completion_rx: ...) -> Self { ... }
}
```

`App::new` always constructs `PersistenceState` via `PersistenceState::new(save_file, save_completion_rx)`. The `Default` impl (which would give all-`None`) is never invoked anywhere in the codebase. Having both is misleading — it suggests `Default` is a valid construction path when in practice `save_completion_rx` needs to come from the state manager and should never silently default to `None`.

**Action:** Remove `#[derive(Default)]`. If `App` ever needs a fully-defaulted construction path (e.g. for tests), the `new(None, None)` call is explicit and clear enough. Alternatively, drop `new()` and use a struct literal in `App::new` — matching how every other sub-struct (`AnimationState::default()`, `FilterState::default()`, etc.) is constructed — but that requires callers to know all field names, so the `new()` constructor is probably worth keeping.

---

## 4. `viewport_height` and `last_frame_area` are redundant in `ViewState`

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/view.rs:11`
**Type:** Cleanup / derived state

```rust
pub struct ViewState {
    pub strategy: Box<dyn ViewStrategy>,
    pub card_list_component: CardListComponent,
    pub viewport_height: usize,   // <-- redundant?
    pub last_frame_area: Rect,    // <-- source of truth
}
```

`last_frame_area` is a `ratatui::layout::Rect` that contains the full terminal dimensions from the most recent render frame. `viewport_height` is a `usize` that is a subset of that information (the height of the card list viewport area, which is derived from the frame area minus borders/padding).

Storing both means there are two sources of truth that can drift: if `last_frame_area` is updated but `viewport_height` is not recomputed, scroll calculations could use a stale height.

**Action:** Audit all reads of `self.view.viewport_height`. If it is always set immediately after a frame render using `last_frame_area`-derived math, consider computing it on-demand via a `ViewState::viewport_height()` method rather than storing it. If it is genuinely updated at a different cadence, document why.

---

## 5. `ANIMATION_DURATION_MS` bypasses the re-export pattern

**File:** `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/animation.rs:6`, `crates/kanban-tui/src/app/mod.rs` (animation tick handler)
**Type:** Consistency / style

Every other symbol from the 12 sub-modules is re-exported at the `app/mod.rs` level:
```rust
pub mod animation;
pub use animation::{AnimationState, CardAnimation};  // types re-exported
// ANIMATION_DURATION_MS is NOT re-exported
```

But the const is accessed internally via its module path:
```rust
if elapsed >= animation::ANIMATION_DURATION_MS {
```

This is the only place in `mod.rs` that reaches back into a sub-module via its path rather than using a re-exported name. It works, but it is inconsistent and slightly surprising — a reader skimming `mod.rs` would not see `ANIMATION_DURATION_MS` in the re-export list and might not realise it is defined in `animation.rs`.

**Option A:** Add `pub use animation::ANIMATION_DURATION_MS;` to the re-export block (makes it consistent, but exports an internal implementation detail at crate level).

**Option B (preferred):** Since this const is only used in one place inside `mod.rs`, make it `pub(super)` in `animation.rs` (currently `pub(crate)`) and access it as `animation::ANIMATION_DURATION_MS` — but add a comment in the re-export block: `// ANIMATION_DURATION_MS intentionally not re-exported (internal use only)`.

**Option C:** Inline the value (`150u128`) directly at the use site and delete the named const — it is only used once.
cards.title: Follow-up nits from PR #189 app.rs decomposition

cards.description:
## Context

`kanban-mcp` currently shells out to the `kanban` CLI binary for all operations (board/column/card/sprint CRUD). This creates two problems:

1. **Build cost**: Nix builds of `kanban-mcp` must also build the full `kanban-cli` binary as a runtime dep.
2. **Storage backend isolation**: When KAN-159 lands, the MCP server cannot participate in pluggable storage — it is locked into whatever store the CLI subprocess uses, with no injection point.

## Relationship to KAN-159

KAN-159 establishes `DynStore = Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>` as the abstraction seam in the TUI. The same pattern needs to apply to `kanban-mcp`. Without this card, MCP is stranded on JSON forever even after SQLite ships.

## Goal

Extract `crates/kanban-cli/src/handlers/` into a new `kanban-service` crate whose functions accept `Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync>`. Both `kanban-cli` and `kanban-mcp` depend on `kanban-service` and wire up their own store instance.

## Target Dependency Graph

```
kanban-cli --+
             +--> kanban-service --> kanban-persistence (PersistenceStore trait)
kanban-mcp --+                            ^              ^
                                   JsonFileStore     SqliteStore
```

## Work Items

- [ ] Create crates/kanban-service/ with handlers migrated from kanban-cli/src/handlers/
- [ ] Handlers accept Arc<dyn PersistenceStore + Send + Sync> instead of reading disk directly
- [ ] Update kanban-cli to depend on kanban-service (no behavioral change)
- [ ] Rewrite kanban-mcp/src/context.rs to call kanban-service directly (replace executor.rs subprocess model)
- [ ] Remove kanban from crates/kanban-mcp/default.nix nativeBuildInputs and postInstall wrap
- [ ] Update kanban-mcp integration tests to not require KANBAN_BIN

## Acceptance Criteria

- kanban-mcp Nix derivation has no dependency on the kanban or kanban-cli binary
- MCP server works with both JSON and SQLite stores (after KAN-159 merges)
- No behavioral regression in CLI or MCP
cards.title: Extract kanban-service crate from kanban-cli handlers

cards.description:
Follow-up to KAN-226 (PR #190): extract kanban-service crate.

## Items

### 1. Make KanbanContext fields private (+ pub(crate) accessors if needed)

Fields currently pub in crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs:
  pub boards: Vec<Board>
  pub columns: Vec<Column>
  pub cards: Vec<Card>
  pub sprints: Vec<Sprint>
  pub archived_cards: Vec<ArchivedCard>
  pub graph: DependencyGraph

External callers confirmed by grep — none access fields directly:
- crates/kanban-cli/src/context.rs: imports KanbanContext, delegates via KanbanOperations methods
- crates/kanban-mcp/src/context.rs: same pattern
- crates/kanban-mcp/tests/integration.rs: uses KanbanContext::load_json() + trait methods only
- crates/kanban-service/tests/persistence.rs: same

The 490 field accesses in kanban-tui are against TuiContext (crates/kanban-tui/src/tui_context.rs), not KanbanContext. No conflict.

KanbanOperations already covers all read paths. Making fields private is a zero-impact change. No new accessors should be needed unless something is missed.

### 2. Make execute() pub(crate)

crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs:
  pub fn execute(&mut self, command: Box<dyn Command>) -> KanbanResult<()>

Only called internally within the same file by KanbanOperations impl methods. No external callers (confirmed by grep across all crates). Exposes the command pattern internals unnecessarily.

Fix: change to pub(crate).

### 3. Make DataSnapshot pub(crate)

crates/kanban-service/src/context.rs:
  pub struct DataSnapshot { ... }

Serialisation detail used only within the crate for save()/load()/import_board()/export_board(). Not part of the public API contract.

Fix: change to pub(crate).

## Non-issue: Uuid::new_v4() in save()

The review flagged PersistenceMetadata::new(Uuid::new_v4()) in KanbanContext::save() as a possible regression vs. the old self.store.instance_id() call. It is not.

JsonFileStore::save() (crates/kanban-persistence/src/store/json_file_store.rs:112) unconditionally overwrites the passed instance_id with its own stable instance_id:
  snapshot.metadata.instance_id = self.instance_id;

Conflict detection uses FileMetadata (file mtime + size on disk), not instance_id. The old call was equally irrelevant. No action needed.

cards.description:
Submit the kanban CLI to the Arch User Repository so Arch users can install via `yay -S kanban`.

No approval needed — push and it goes live immediately.

## Prerequisites

- AUR account at https://aur.archlinux.org
- SSH key registered in AUR profile:
  ```
  ssh-keygen -f ~/.ssh/aur
  # add contents of ~/.ssh/aur.pub to AUR account > My Account > SSH keys
  ```
- Add to ~/.ssh/config:
  ```
  Host aur.archlinux.org
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/aur
    User aur
  ```

## Package Name

Use `kanban` — not taken in official repos, `kanban-bin` is taken but for a different project (GitLab kanban). Plain source build gets the plain name.

## PKGBUILD

Create a `PKGBUILD` file:

```bash
# Maintainer: Your Name <email>
pkgname=kanban
pkgver=0.3.5
pkgrel=1
pkgdesc="Terminal-based kanban board with MCP server integration"
arch=("x86_64")
url="https://github.com/youruser/kanban"
license=("MIT")
makedepends=("rust" "cargo")
source=("$pkgname-$pkgver.tar.gz::https://github.com/youruser/kanban/archive/v$pkgver.tar.gz")
sha256sums=("SKIP")

build() {
    cd "$pkgname-$pkgver"
    cargo build --release --locked --bin kanban
}

package() {
    cd "$pkgname-$pkgver"
    install -Dm755 "target/release/kanban" "$pkgdir/usr/bin/kanban"
    install -Dm644 LICENSE "$pkgdir/usr/share/licenses/$pkgname/LICENSE"
}
```

Replace sha256sums SKIP with the real hash once the release tarball exists:
```
curl -L <tarball-url> | sha256sum
```

## Submission

```bash
git clone ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/kanban.git
cd kanban
cp /path/to/PKGBUILD .
makepkg --printsrcinfo > .SRCINFO
git add PKGBUILD .SRCINFO
git commit -m "Initial import"
git push
```

## Ongoing Maintenance

- Update pkgver + sha256sums on each release, regenerate .SRCINFO, push
- Watch AUR comments for bug reports
- If you stop maintaining, disown via the AUR web UI


AUR auto-publish workflow
---

 After each GitHub release of kanban, the AUR package at aur.archlinux.org/kanban needs to be updated with the new version and sha256. This should be fully automated via a separate GitHub Actions workflow — decoupled from the release job so a failure doesn't block a
 release.

 No shared scripts, no flake inputs between repos. Just a standalone workflow with ~20 lines of shell.

 Files to create

 .github/workflows/aur-publish.yml (new, in main kanban repo)

 Triggered by release: published. Clones the AUR repo, updates PKGBUILD and .SRCINFO, commits and pushes.

```yaml
 name: Publish to AUR

 on:
   release:
     types: [published]

 jobs:
   aur-publish:
     name: Update AUR package
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
       - uses: cachix/install-nix-action@v27
         with:
           github_access_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
           extra_nix_config: |
             experimental-features = nix-command flakes

       - name: Setup AUR SSH key
         run: |
           mkdir -p ~/.ssh
           echo "${{ secrets.AUR_SSH_KEY }}" > ~/.ssh/aur_key
           chmod 600 ~/.ssh/aur_key
           ssh-keyscan aur.archlinux.org >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts

       - name: Clone AUR repo
         env:
           GIT_SSH_COMMAND: "ssh -i ~/.ssh/aur_key -o IdentitiesOnly=yes"
         run: |
           git clone ssh://aur@aur.archlinux.org/kanban.git /tmp/aur-kanban

       - name: Update PKGBUILD and .SRCINFO
         run: |
           VERSION="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
           VERSION="${VERSION#v}"
           TARBALL_URL="https://github.com/fulsomenko/kanban/archive/v${VERSION}.tar.gz"
           SHA256=$(curl -sL "$TARBALL_URL" | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}')

           cd /tmp/aur-kanban
           sed -i "s/^pkgver=.*/pkgver=${VERSION}/" PKGBUILD
           sed -i "s/^pkgrel=.*/pkgrel=1/" PKGBUILD
           sed -i "s/sha256sums=.*/sha256sums=(\"${SHA256}\")/" PKGBUILD

           MAKEPKG_CONF=$(find /nix/store -name "makepkg.conf" -path "*/pacman-*/etc/*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
           nix-shell -p pacman libarchive --run \
             "makepkg --printsrcinfo --config ${MAKEPKG_CONF}" 2>/dev/null \
             | grep -v "^Kanban\|^📦\|^🦀" > .SRCINFO

       - name: Commit and push
         env:
           GIT_SSH_COMMAND: "ssh -i ~/.ssh/aur_key -o IdentitiesOnly=yes"
         run: |
           cd /tmp/aur-kanban
           git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
           git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
           git add PKGBUILD .SRCINFO
           git commit -m "Update to ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
           git push
```

 Secret setup

 1. Generate key

 ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /path/to/key -C "github-actions kanban AUR" -N ""

 2. Store in sops-nix

 Add the private key to your sops secrets and activate it via NixOS. Path managed by sops.

 3. Register public key on AUR

 1. cat /path/to/key.pub
 2. https://aur.archlinux.org → My Account → SSH Public Key → paste and save

 4. Add to GitHub Actions secrets

 gh secret set AUR_SSH_KEY --repo fulsomenko/kanban < <(sops -d path/to/encrypted/aur-key)

 Required secret name: AUR_SSH_KEY

 Verification

 After merging and setting the secret:
 1. Publish a release (or re-publish an existing one) to trigger the workflow
 2. Pull ~/fulsomenko/aur/kanban and check for a new commit
 3. Verify AUR page reflects the version
